Ayahs

كٓهيعٓصٓ (۱)
Kaf, Ha, Ya, ‘Ayn, Suad. (1)
ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبْدَهُۥ زَكَرِيَّآ (۲)
[This is] an account of your Lord’s mercy on His servant, Zechariah, (2)
إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ نِدَآءً خَفِيًّا (۳)
when he called out to his Lord with a secret cry. (3)

Key Points

"كهیعص" (Kāf Hā Yā ‘Ayn Ṣād) is one of the disconnected letters (al-ḥurūf al-muqaṭṭa‘ah) of the Qur’an, in which secrets and mysteries are hidden. In some supplications, we swear by these letters to Allah, saying: "By the right of كهیعص," just as Imam Ali عليه السلام said in a supplication: "O Allah, we ask You, O Kāf, Hā, Yā, ‘Ayn, Ṣād." (3)

Nidā’ (call) means calling out with sound and shouting, while khafiyyan means secretly, not softly, because such a call cannot be soft, but it can be secret. (4)

Perhaps Zachariah’s secret supplication was due to fear of people’s mockery, lest they say why this old man is asking Allah for a child.

The name "Zachariah" (زكریّا) appears seven times in the Qur’an. He was one of the Prophets of the Children of Israel and a descendant of Aaron, the brother of Moses.

It is stated in a tradition: "خیر الدّعا الخفىّ" (The best supplication is the secret one). The best supplication is that which is secret. (5)

3) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān. 4) Tafsīr Namūneh. 5) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Remembering Allah’s special favors is a factor in Divine love. "ذكر رحمت ربّك" (The mention of the mercy of your Lord)

2- Remembering the friends of Allah is a virtue. "ذكر... زكریّا" (The mention of... Zachariah)

3- Worship and supplication are the grounds for receiving mercy. "رحمت ربّك... اذ نادى ربّه" (The mercy of your Lord... when he called upon his Lord)

4- We should not overlook the role of supplication in the transformations of human life. "اذ نادى" (When he called). Even the Prophets resorted to supplication to solve their problems.

5- A believer never feels lonely. "اذ نادى ربّه" (When he called upon his Lord)

6- If Allah wills, He makes the secret call of His sincere servants public for history. "نداءً خَفیّا" (A secret call)

7- Secret supplication is more valuable because it is closer to sincerity (Ikhlāṣ). "نداءً خفیّا" (A secret call)

Explanations

Portrait of Sūrat Maryam. Sūrat Maryam is the nineteenth Sūrah of the Qur’an, containing ninety-eight verses, and was revealed in Mecca. (1)

The content of the Sūrah, like other Meccan Sūrahs, consists of issues related to the Day of Judgment, the fate of the criminals, the reward of the righteous, and parts of the stories of Zachariah, Mary, Jesus son of Mary, John (Yaḥyā), Abraham, Ishmael, and Idris علیهم السلام.

We read in Tafsīr al-Mīzān: Allah states in the last two verses of this Sūrah that His goal in revealing this Sūrah is to give glad tidings and warnings, and this goal is presented in the beautiful framework of the stories of the Prophets.

The name Maryam (Mary) is mentioned 34 times in the Qur’an. Allah has not mentioned the name of any woman by her specific name in the Qur’an except Maryam, and the only Sūrah in the Qur’an named after a woman is this Sūrat Maryam.

Regarding the virtue of reciting this Sūrah, it is stated that whoever persists in reciting this Sūrah will not depart from this world until Allah makes him self-sufficient in terms of soul, wealth, and children. (2) Of course, there is no doubt that by acting upon the content of this Sūrah, a person will find himself independent of others.

1) Some consider verses 58 and 71 to be Medinan. 2) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى وَهَنَ ٱلْعَظْمُ مِنِّى وَٱشْتَعَلَ ٱلرَّأْسُ شَيْبًا وَلَمْ أَكُنۢ بِدُعَآئِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّا (٤)
He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed my bones have become feeble, and my head has turned white with age, yet never have I, my Lord, been disappointed in supplicating You! (4)
وَإِنِّى خِفْتُ ٱلْمَوَٰلِىَ مِن وَرَآءِى وَكَانَتِ ٱمْرَأَتِى عَاقِرًا فَهَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا (٥)
Indeed I fear my kinsmen, after me, and my wife is barren. So grant me from Yourself an heir (5)

Key Points

‘Āqir (عاقِر) refers to a man or woman from whom no child is born. Ishti'āl (اشتعال) means the blazing up of fire and its spread. Therefore, the phrase wa-shta'ala ar-ra'su (واشتَعل الرّأس) means that the whiteness of the hair on my head has blazed up and spread like fire. Considering Ayah 38 of Āl 'Imrān, which quotes Zachariah's prayer thus: "رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً" (My Lord, grant me from Yourself a good offspring), it becomes clear that the intent of waliyyan (ولیّاً) in this Ayah is a righteous son who will be a successor to his father. In supplication (du'a), sometimes the relationship between the servant and Allah becomes so intimate that instead of saying Yā Rabb (O Lord), the servant says Rabb (Lord). (6) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Zachariah's concern was regarding his uncles and cousins. (7)

6) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an. 7) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- In supplication, seek help using the word Rabb (Lord): "رَبِّ... رَبِّ... رَبِّ"; mention your weaknesses: "وَاشْتَعَلَ الرَّأْسُ شَيْبًا" (and the head has flared up with gray hair); mention Allah's blessings and favors: "وَلَمْ أَكُن بِدُعَائِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّا" (and I have never been, by my prayer to You, my Lord, unhappy); and then voice your need: "فَهَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا" (so grant me from Yourself an heir).

2- The foundation of the human body is the bone, and the weakening of the bone leads to the weakening of the entire body. "وَهَنَ الْعَظْمُ" (the bone has weakened).

3- A believer never despairs of Allah's Mercy. (Zachariah asks Allah for a child in his old age.) "رَبِّ إِنِّي وَهَنَ الْعَظْمُ... وَلَمْ أَكُن بِدُعَائِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّا" (My Lord, indeed the bone has weakened... and I have never been, by my prayer to You, my Lord, unhappy).

4- A despairing person is wretched and unhappy. "وَلَمْ أَكُن بِدُعَائِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّا" (and I have never been, by my prayer to You, my Lord, unhappy). Even in the most difficult circumstances, despair finds no way into the hearts of the men of God.

5- The men of God fear a bad heir. "خِفْتُ الْمَوَالِيَ مِن وَرَائِي" (I fear my relatives after me).

6- In the divine perspective, the Will of Allah governs natural factors. "هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ" (grant me from Yourself).

7- Although Allah knows human needs, supplication and lamentation contain blessings such as spiritual tranquility, the humility and servitude of the supplicant, and the praise and glorification of Allah. "إِنِّي وَهَنَ الْعَظْمُ... هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا" (Indeed, the bone has weakened... grant me from Yourself an heir).

8- A child is a heavenly gift (hiba) from Allah. "فَهَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ" (so grant me from Yourself).

9- Neither abandon a barren wife nor lose hope. "وَكَانَتِ امْرَأَتِي عَاقِرًا... هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا" (and my wife has been barren... grant me from Yourself an heir).

10- The desire for permanence and eternity exists in human nature, and man does not like to be forgotten or cut off (without progeny). "هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا" (grant me from Yourself an heir).

11- Although one of man's concerns is being childless ("خِفْتُ" - I fear), the friends of Allah desire a child primarily for the continuation of their spiritual goals. "وَلِيًّا" (an heir/successor).

Ayahs

يَرِثُنِى وَيَرِثُ مِنْ ءَالِ يَعْقُوبَ ۖ وَٱجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّا (٦)
who may inherit from me and inherit from the House of Jacob, and make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You]!’ (6)
يَـٰزَكَرِيَّآ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَـٰمٍ ٱسْمُهُۥ يَحْيَىٰ لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّا (۷)
‘O Zechariah! Indeed We give you the good news of a son, whose name is ‘‘John.’’ Never before have We made anyone his namesake.’ (7)
قَالَ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِى غُلَـٰمٌ وَكَانَتِ ٱمْرَأَتِى عَاقِرًا وَقَدْ بَلَغْتُ مِنَ ٱلْكِبَرِ عِتِيًّا (۸)
He said, ‘My Lord! How shall I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I am already advanced in age?’ (8)

Key Points

In the verses of the Quran, sometimes the meaning of inheritance (irth) is knowledge: «و اَورثنا بنى اسرائیل الكتاب» (8), and sometimes it is governance/rule: «و ورث سلیمان داود» (9). However, wherever there is no specific evidence (to the contrary), the meaning of inheritance is wealth (mal). Just as in these verses, the meaning of «یَرِثُنى» is a child who inherits from his father (10). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Allah's inheritance from the believing servant is a righteous child who worships God after his death. Then he recited this verse (11). The word «رَضى» means both pleasing to Allah (marḍiyy) and beloved by the people. The station of Allah's satisfaction with man, and man's satisfaction with Allah, is the highest of stations and the great triumph (fawz al-'aẓīm). The phrase «مِن قبل سَمیّا» either means that the name Yahya is unprecedented, or that his personal characteristics are unprecedented. «عِتیّا» means exceeding the limit (going beyond bounds) and is said about someone whose limbs have become withered due to old age. Zachariah's question, when he said: «أنّى یكون لى غلام» means: Will my wife and I become young again, or will we have a child while remaining in this state of old age? (12) Yes, the knowledge of the Prophets is also limited, and expressing surprise at something does not contradict their station of prophethood. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: There was a five-year interval between the glad tidings of the child and the birth of Yahya (13).

8) Ghafir, 53. 9) Naml, 16. 10) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi. 11) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 12) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi. 13) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan; Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 176.

Messages

1- The sanctity of prophethood does not contradict natural and instinctive desires, such as the desire for a child. «یَرثُنى»

2- Following the path of the Prophets is a spiritual value. «یَرِثنى»

3- Prophets, like ordinary people, leave an inheritance. «یَرثُنى»

4- Men of God pay attention to spiritual values even in their material needs. «یرثنى... رضیّاً»

5- If the offspring is pleasing to Allah, it is a mercy. «واجعله ربّ رضیّا» In Ayah 2, we read: «ذكر رحمت ربّك عبده زكریّا»

6- One must pray for the righteousness and goodness of the child, even before conception. «و اجعله ربّ رضیّا»

7- We should ask Allah for a righteous child. «واجعله ربّ رضیّا»

8- We should not take naming lightly; some names have been revealed by Allah. «نبشِّرك بغلام اسمه یحیى» (A name is a sign of a person's beliefs, culture, interests, affiliations, goals, aspirations, and viewpoints.)

9- Barren men and women should not despair of Divine grace. «اِنّا نُبشّرك بغلام... عاقراً... عتیّاً»

10- Whether the child is a boy or a girl is in the hands of Allah. «نبشّرك بغلام»

Ayahs

قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ رَبُّكَ هُوَ عَلَىَّ هَيِّنٌ وَقَدْ خَلَقْتُكَ مِن قَبْلُ وَلَمْ تَكُ شَيْـًٔا (۹)
He said, ‘So shall it be. Your Lord has said, ‘‘It is simple for Me.’’ Certainly I created you before when you were nothing.’ (9)
قَالَ رَبِّ ٱجْعَل لِّىٓ ءَايَةً ۚ قَالَ ءَايَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ ٱلنَّاسَ ثَلَـٰثَ لَيَالٍ سَوِيًّا (۱٠)
He said, ‘My Lord! Appoint a sign for me.’ He said, ‘Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three complete nights.’ (10)

Key Points

Allah is capable of everything, and every task is easy for Him. As He says in this Ayah: «هو علىّ هیّن» (This matter is easy for Me). We also read in Ayah 22 of Al-Hadid and Ayah 7 of At-Taghabun: «ذلك على اللّه یسیر» (That is easy for Allah). And in Ayah 20 and 106 of Al-Baqarah, it states: «اِنّ اللّه على كلّ شیى ء قدیر» (Indeed, Allah is over all things competent). And in Ayah 45 of Al-Kahf, He says: «و كان اللّه على كلّ شیى ء مقتدرا» (And Allah is over all things competent). Allah is powerful over everything. Sometimes, the intent of "night" (شب) in common Arabic usage is a full day (24 hours).(14) Therefore, we read in Ayah 41 of Al ‘Imran: «ثلاثة ایّام» (three days), while here He says: «ثلاث لیال» (three nights). This is because sometimes night and day are mentioned interchangeably. In the commentaries Majma‘ al-Bayan, Tabari, and Al-Mizan, it is mentioned regarding Hadrat Zakariyya (عليه السلام) that his tongue would loosen when reciting the divine verses and remembering Allah, but it would become tied when speaking to people. Hadrat Zakariyya requested a sign from Allah to distinguish between truth and falsehood, between divine revelation and satanic delusions.(15)

14) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 15) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Natural laws and material causes never limit the power of Allah. «هو علىّ هیّن»

2- Reflection upon creation and phenomena removes all doubt and skepticism regarding Allah's power. «خلقتك... و لم تك شیئا»

3- The Prophets sought to reach higher stages of certainty (Yaqin). Abraham asked Allah for a sign so that «لیطمئنّ قلبى» (my heart may be assured).(16) Zakariyya also requested a specific sign from Allah. «اجعل لى آیة»

4- All our actions are by Divine Will; if He does not will it, we lack the power to speak even with complete health. «لا تكلّم الناس»

16) Al-Baqarah, 260.

Ayah

فَخَرَجَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلْمِحْرَابِ فَأَوْحَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِمْ أَن سَبِّحُوا۟ بُكْرَةً وَعَشِيًّا (۱۱)
So he emerged before his people from the Temple, and signalled to them that they should glorify [Allah] morning and evening. (11)

Key Points

«بُكرة» refers to the time between the break of dawn (Fajr) and sunrise, and «عَشیّاً» refers either to the period between noon and sunset or the end of the day. The Mihrab (sanctuary/niche) is called Mihrab because it is the place of struggle (war) against Satan and the temptations that prevent human concentration. Perhaps the Mihrabs of the Children of Israel were the mosques that people chose for worship and where they performed their devotions. According to Tabarsi and Fakhr Razi, the intent of Tasbih (glorification) in this verse is Salat (prayer), because prayer includes glorification.

Messages

  1. In propagation (Dawah), the preacher's shared nationality/kinship with the audience is a priority. «فخرج على قومه»
  2. Men of God designate a specific place for their acts of worship. «المحراب»
  3. The one who invites people to worship must himself be devoted to worship. «فخرج على قومه من المحراب فاوحى الیهم»
  4. Propagation must be carried out by any means possible. (When Zechariah's tongue was tied, he instructed the people by gesture to glorify Allah.) «فاوحى الیهم»
  5. The duty of the prophets is to invite people to worship. «فاوحى الیهم ان سبّحوا»
  6. The elderly man having a child from a barren wife is a sign that Allah is free from every deficiency and inability. «سبّحوا»
  7. Worship is valuable when it is continuous, not temporary or seasonal. «بكرة و عشیّا»

Ayahs

يَـٰيَحْيَىٰ خُذِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِقُوَّةٍ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا (۱۲)
‘O John!’ [We said,] ‘Hold on with power to the Book!’ And We gave him judgement while still a child, (12)
وَحَنَانًا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَوٰةً ۖ وَكَانَ تَقِيًّا (۱۳)
and a compassion and purity from Us. He was Godwary (13)

Key Points

Hukm (حكم) has been interpreted to mean knowledge and understanding (17) and the power of judgment (18). In Tafsir al-Mizan, we read that the intent of hukm is knowledge of Divine teachings and the lifting of the veil of the unseen. Three individuals attained prophethood in childhood: Sulayman, 'Isa (Jesus), and Yahya (John) علیهم السلام. And three individuals also attained Imamate in childhood: Imam Jawad, Imam Hadi, and Imam Mahdi علیهم السلام (19). Some have said that Yahya علیه السلام is named John (Yuhanna) in the New Testament. Perhaps the intent of the heavenly Book here is the Torah, because no other Book revealed to Prophet Yahya is known. This command also appears in Ayahs 63 and 93 of Surah Al-Baqarah: that you should take the heavenly Book with strength. «خذوا ما آتیناكم بقوّة» (Take what We have given you with strength). Hanan (حنان) means compassion and kindness. Allah's mercy towards Yahya, Yahya's love for Allah, the people's affection for Yahya, and Yahya's love for the people are all reflections of the Divine «حناناً من لدنّا» (a compassion from Us). Everything in the path of Allah is mercy: its God is Ar-Rahman (the Most Compassionate), its Prophet is «رحمة للعالمین» (a mercy to the worlds) (20) and «حناناً من لّدنّا» (a compassion from Us), its Book is «رحمة للعالمین» (a mercy to the worlds) (21), and its people are «رحماء بینهم» (merciful among themselves) (22). Zakat (زكات) has been interpreted to mean charity, growth, purity from sin, and blessing. Yahya was a blessed gift for Prophet Zakariyya and an example of a pure and developed human being.

17) Lisan al-Arab. 18) Qamus al-Quran. 19) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan. 20) Al-Anbiya, 107. 21) Al-Isra, 82. 22) Al-Fath, 29.

Messages

1- The leader must possess firmness in realizing the goals of the heavenly Book. «یا یحیى خُذ الكتاب بقوّة» (O Yahya! Take the Book with strength).

2- Preserving the religion and implementing its rulings requires decisiveness and strength. «خُذ الكتاب بقوّة» (Take the Book with strength).

3- Religious adherence must be accompanied by strength and seriousness. «خُذ الكتاب بقوّة» (Take the Book with strength).

4- If Allah wills, He grants the status of prophethood even to a child. «آتیناه الحكم صبیّا» (We granted him wisdom while yet a child).

5- The prerequisites for propagation and nurturing are compassion, affection, and purity. «و حَناناً و زكاة» (and compassion and purity).

6- The source of compassion and affection is Allah. «من لّدنّا» (from Us).

7- The spirit of compassion and kindness is a virtue for humanity. «حَناناً» (compassion).

8- Piety is valuable when it is constant and a characteristic of the person. «كان تقیّاً» (He was pious).

9- Piety does not mean isolation or harshness; rather, it means being among the people with kindness and affection. «حَناناً... كان تقیّاً» (compassion... He was pious).

10- Piety is the ground for receiving Divine favors, growth, and popularity. «حَنانا...زكاة... كان تقیّاً» (compassion... purity... He was pious).

Ayahs

وَبَرًّۢا بِوَٰلِدَيْهِ وَلَمْ يَكُن جَبَّارًا عَصِيًّا (۱٤)
and good to his parents, and was not self-willed or disobedient. (14)
وَسَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا (۱٥)
Peace be to him, the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he is raised alive! (15)

Key Points

In these verses, Allah enumerates various characteristics regarding Prophet Yahya (John): He was pious in the presence of Allah, «تَقیّا» (pious); He was kind to his parents, «بَرّاً» (dutiful/kind); and He was not arrogant or rebellious towards people.(23) «لم یكن جبّاراً عَصیّا» The intent of safety (peace/salvation) at the time of death is the soundness of belief and dying with faith.

23) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Status and position should not make us neglectful of our parents; even a Prophet must be kind to his parents. «و بَرّاً بوالدیه»

2- Kindness to parents is a manifestation of Divine piety. «كان تقیّاً و بَرّا بوالدیه»

3- The kindness to parents that is praiseworthy is that which contains no disobedience or tyranny. «بَرّاً بوالدیه ولم یكن جبّاراً عَصیّا»

4- Abandoning kindness to parents is a sign of rebellion and arrogance. «ولم یكن جبّاراً عَصیّا»

5- Sending salutations and greetings upon the friends of Allah is not restricted to the time of their life. «سلام علیه یوم ولد و یوم یموت و یوم ...»

6- Giving salutations is not a sign of insignificance, because the Great God also gives salutations. «سلام علیه», «سلام على ابراهیم»(24) and «سلام على موسى و هارون»(25)

7- Yahya traversed all ups and downs with the soundness of religion and faith in Allah. «سلام علیه یوم ولد و یوم یموت و ...»

8- Honoring the day of birth and commemorating the day of death of the friends of Allah is a Quranic matter. «سلام علیه یوم ولد و یوم یموت و ...»

9- Reciting the Ziyarat Namah (pilgrimage supplication), which is a salutation upon the friends of Allah throughout their lives, is a Quranic matter. «سلام علیه یوم ولد و یوم یموت و ...»

10- The key to living soundly, dying soundly, and safety in the Hereafter is piety (taqwa), kindness to parents, and avoidance of sin and transgression. «كان تقیّاً و برّاً بوالدیه و لم یكن جباراً عَصیّا سلام علیه یوم...» 24) Saffat, 109. 25) Saffat, 120.

Ayahs

وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَرْيَمَ إِذِ ٱنتَبَذَتْ مِنْ أَهْلِهَا مَكَانًا شَرْقِيًّا (۱٦)
And mention in the Book Mary, when she withdrew from her family to an easterly place. (16)
فَٱتَّخَذَتْ مِن دُونِهِمْ حِجَابًا فَأَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْهَا رُوحَنَا فَتَمَثَّلَ لَهَا بَشَرًا سَوِيًّا (۱۷)
Thus did she seclude herself from them, whereupon We sent to her Our Spirit and he became incarnate for her as a well-proportioned human. (17)

Key Points

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Maryam (Mary) is one of the four exemplary women. (26) Nadh (نَبذ) means to cast something away out of disregard for it, and intibadh (انتباذ) refers to withdrawing from people. Since Maryam had been dedicated to the service of Al-Aqsa Mosque, she had chosen a corner in the east of the mosque for herself.

26) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 201.

Messages

1- One of the duties of religious leaders is to honor the pure figures of history. "واذكر ..." (And mention...)

2- The history of heroic women, just like the history of great men, must be kept alive. "واذكر فى الكتاب مریم" (And mention in the Book Maryam), "واذكر فى الكتاب ابراهیم" (And mention in the Book Abraham). (27)

3- We should choose a secluded place for worship. "فاتّخذت من دونهم حجاباً" (So she took, apart from them, a screen/veil.)

4- Worship is the context for the descent of Divine favors. "فاتّخذت - فارسلنا" (So she took [a screen] - Then We sent [Our Spirit].)

5- Separating and hanging a curtain in a place where women worship is a virtue. "فاتّخذت من دونهم حجاباً" (So she took, apart from them, a screen/veil.) Maryam had hung a curtain in a corner of the mosque for worship.

6- Gabriel (Jibril) holds a high status with Allah. "روحنا" (Our Spirit)

7- Angels can take a form that humans can see without changing their essential nature. "فتمثّل لها بشراً" (Then He appeared to her as a man.)

8- Sometimes, to carry out certain missions, one must change appearance. "فتمثّل لها" (Then He appeared to her.)

9- Education (or Divine guidance) must be step-by-step. Allah first recounted the story of the birth of Yahya (John) from elderly parents, and then He described the birth of Isa (Jesus) without a father. "فتمثّل لها" (Then He appeared to her.)

10- Gabriel (Jibril) also visits those who are not Prophets. "فتمثّل لها" (Then He appeared to her.)

11- A woman can reach a status where an angel communicates with her. "فتمثّل لها" (Then He appeared to her.)

12- The power and will of Allah are not confined within the framework of means and causes, but rather prevail over them. "ارسلنا الیها روحنا فتمثّل لها بشرا" (We sent to her Our Spirit, and He appeared to her as a man.)

27) Maryam, 41.

Ayahs

قَالَتْ إِنِّىٓ أَعُوذُ بِٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ مِنكَ إِن كُنتَ تَقِيًّا (۱۸)
She said, ‘I seek the protection of the All-beneficent from you, should you be Godwary!’ (18)
قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ رَسُولُ رَبِّكِ لِأَهَبَ لَكِ غُلَـٰمًا زَكِيًّا (۱۹)
He said, ‘I am only a messenger of your Lord that I may give you a pure son.’ (19)
قَالَتْ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِى غُلَـٰمٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِى بَشَرٌ وَلَمْ أَكُ بَغِيًّا (۲٠)
She said, ‘How shall I have a child seeing that no human being has ever touched me, nor have I been unchaste?’ (20)

Key Points

«Zaki» (زَكىّ) derives from the root «Zakwah» (زكوة), meaning purity (ṭahārat), growth (numū), and blessing (barakah). And «Baghiyyan» (بَغیّاً) here means an adulteress/fornicator. In this Surah, the issue of Allah granting a child as a gift (hībah) is mentioned repeatedly: in Ayah 19, the gift of Jesus to Mary; in Ayah 49, the gift of Isaac and Jacob to Abraham; in Ayah 53, the gift of Aaron to Moses; and in Ayah 7, the glad tidings of a child to Zachariah (Zakariyya) are presented.

Messages

1- Chaste individuals tremble at the feeling of potential sin and seek refuge in Allah. «Qālat innī a‘ūdhu ...»

2- The best refuge is Divine Mercy. «A‘ūdhu bi-r-Raḥmān»

3- Seeking refuge (isti‘ādhah) and turning to the Exalted God is one of Allah’s recommendations to the Prophets and is the practice (sīrah) of the Prophets and the Divine Saints.(28) «A‘ūdhu bi-r-Raḥmān»

4- One should not intrude upon the privacy of individuals unannounced. «A‘ūdhu bi-r-Raḥmān minka»

5- A woman being alone in seclusion (khalwah) with a non-mahram man is dangerous. «Innī a‘ūdhu bi-r-Raḥmān minka»

6- In forbidding evil (nahy ‘an al-munkar), utilize the religious zeal (ghayrah) of individuals. «In kunta taqiyyā»

7- Piety (Taqwa) is the path to chastity and a deterrent against sin. «In kunta taqiyyā»

8- Introducing oneself to dispel accusations and public concern is necessary. «Innamā anā rasūlu Rabbik»

9- Angels are not merely commissioned to convey Divine Revelation (waḥy); they are also the intermediaries for His actions. «Rasūlu Rabbika li-ahaba laki ghulāman»

10- Having a child is valuable, but the purity of the child is even higher. «Li-ahaba laki ghulāman zakiyyā»

11- Adultery/fornication (zinā) is a detested act in all heavenly religions. «Wa lam aku baghiyyā»

(28) The words «a‘ūdhu», «fast‘idh», and «‘udhtu» in the Quran express this point.

Ayahs

قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَىَّ هَيِّنٌ ۖ وَلِنَجْعَلَهُۥٓ ءَايَةً لِّلنَّاسِ وَرَحْمَةً مِّنَّا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرًا مَّقْضِيًّا (۲۱)
He said, ‘So shall it be. Your Lord says, ‘‘It is simple for Me.’’ And so that We may make him a sign for mankind and a mercy from Us, and it is a matter [already] decided.’ (21)
۞ فَحَمَلَتْهُ فَٱنتَبَذَتْ بِهِۦ مَكَانًا قَصِيًّا (۲۲)
Thus she conceived him, then withdrew with him to a distant place. (22)
فَأَجَآءَهَا ٱلْمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذْعِ ٱلنَّخْلَةِ قَالَتْ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى مِتُّ قَبْلَ هَـٰذَا وَكُنتُ نَسْيًا مَّنسِيًّا (۲۳)
The birth pangs brought her to the trunk of a date palm. She said, ‘I wish I had died before this and become a forgotten thing, beyond recall.’ (23)

Key Points

The Divine Angel appeared to Mary in human form, breathed into her, and she became pregnant. Then, Mary went to a distant place, either due to worry about the accusations of those who were not understanding, or because a pregnant woman needs more tranquility and a calm environment. There is much discussion in the commentaries regarding that place, how Mary became pregnant, and who was the first person to know about this event. However, since the Quran has not mentioned anything about these matters, we will not discuss them either, as this information plays no role in the development of our understanding.

Sometimes the best chosen ones of Allah have endured the greatest hardships. Some of the difficulties and hardships that Lady Mary endured include: A: Accusations and suspicion from the people. B: Pregnancy and childbirth in solitude and estrangement. C: Lacking a place of rest and taking refuge beside a date palm tree. D: Raising a fatherless child amidst the harsh treatment and bad looks of the people.

Messages

1- Allah is the ruler over natural means and causes. «هو علىّ هیّن»

2- The manner of Jesus’s birth is His first miracle. «و لنجعله آیة للناس»

3- Performing extraordinary deeds and miracles is among the affairs of Allah’s Lordship (Rububiyyah) and is in the path of human nurturing. «ربّك»

4- The existence of Jesus is a mercy. One whose existence revives faith in Allah among people and whose work is the guidance and instruction of humanity is certainly a mercy. «رحمةً منّا»

5- No one can prevent the work of Allah. «و كان أمراً مقضیّاً»

6- Sometimes pure individuals must distance themselves from people to avoid their accusations. «مكاناً قصیّاً»

7- Sometimes difficulties are a means for finding paths of goodness. The difficulty and anxiety of childbirth become the context for finding fresh ripe dates. «جذع النّخلة»

8- Sometimes a person cannot even tell his family the secret of his heart and wishes for death. «یا لیتنى مِتّ قبل هذا»

9- Dying is better than living with infamy. «یالیتنى متّ قبل هذا»

Ayahs

فَنَادَىٰهَا مِن تَحْتِهَآ أَلَّا تَحْزَنِى قَدْ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحْتَكِ سَرِيًّا (۲٤)
Thereupon he called her from below her [saying,] ‘Do not grieve! Your Lord has made a spring to flow at your feet. (24)
وَهُزِّىٓ إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ ٱلنَّخْلَةِ تُسَـٰقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا (۲٥)
Shake the trunk of the palm tree, freshly picked dates will drop upon you. (25)

Key Points

The word sariyyā (سَریّا) means a small stream, and janiyyā (جنیّاً) refers to a fruit that is ripe and ready to be picked. Some have said that the caller was Gabriel, but the context of the verses indicates that the caller was Jesus, so that the mother would hear His words and believe, and then, with a peaceful mind, tell the people to ask the infant in the cradle about her chastity. Women during and after childbirth need tranquility, water, and suitable food, which is alluded to in these verses. The food recommended by the Quran and narrations for a woman who has given birth is fresh dates. As the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The first food for women who have given birth should be fresh ripe dates (rutab).(29) And Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The best food for a pregnant woman is fresh dates, and no medicine is a better remedy for her than dates.(30) Alas for the Divine test! On the day when Mary was healthy, heavenly food (mā'idah) was sent down for her, but today, when she is pregnant and alone, she must shake the date palm tree to obtain food.

29) Kāfī, vol. 6, p. 22. 30) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Khiṣāl al-Ṣadūq, p. 637.

Messages

1- Divine inspirations are a guide for humans during times of anxiety. «فناداها»

2- For a chaste woman, no sorrow is greater than the sorrow of being accused of immorality. «لا تحزنى»

3- Excitement and anxiety are harmful to a pregnant woman. «لا تحزنى»

4- Humans must strive to earn sustenance even in the most difficult circumstances. «هزّى»

5- Tranquility precedes nutrition. «لا تحزنى، رُطبا جنیّا» because an anxious person does not desire even the best foods.

Ayah

فَكُلِى وَٱشْرَبِى وَقَرِّى عَيْنًا ۖ فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ ٱلْبَشَرِ أَحَدًا فَقُولِىٓ إِنِّى نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ صَوْمًا فَلَنْ أُكَلِّمَ ٱلْيَوْمَ إِنسِيًّا (۲٦)
Eat, drink, and be comforted. Then if you see any human, say, ‘‘Indeed I have vowed a fast to the All-beneficent, so I will not speak to any human today.’’ ’ (26)

Key Points

The silent fast (the vow of silence) of Lady Maryam (Mary) before the people was either because they lacked the capacity to receive the answer, or because conversing with them would have a negative impact, or because continuing the conversation and answering would lead them to raise further questions and excuses. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Fasting is not only abstaining from eating and drinking, and then He recited this Ayah. (31) It is possible that this Ayah is a continuation of the speech of Prophet Isa (Jesus) to his mother in the previous Ayah.

31) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- A child is a source of comfort (light of the eye) and a cause for man's hope in divine favors. «قَرّى عینا»

2- The best sustenance is that which is accompanied by a sense of peace and joy. «كُلى واشربى و قَرّى عینا»

3- Vows (Nadhir) have a very long history, and one can resort to making a vow to exit certain dead ends. «فقولى اِنّى نذرت»

4- In making a vow, the intention of seeking closeness (Qurbah) is necessary, and the Name of Allah must be mentioned. «نذرتُ للرّحمن»

5- The intention of seeking closeness (Qurbah) does not contradict the intention of seeking relief from difficulties. «نذرتُ للرّحمن» (So that people do not question me regarding the newborn.)

6- Fulfilling a vow is obligatory. «نذرتُ... فلن اكلّم»

7- Sometimes one must remain silent in the face of people's talk. «فلن اُكلّم الیوم اِنسیّا»

Ayahs

فَأَتَتْ بِهِۦ قَوْمَهَا تَحْمِلُهُۥ ۖ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمَرْيَمُ لَقَدْ جِئْتِ شَيْـًٔا فَرِيًّا (۲۷)
Then carrying him she brought him to her people. They said, ‘O Mary, you have certainly come up with an odd thing! (27)
يَـٰٓأُخْتَ هَـٰرُونَ مَا كَانَ أَبُوكِ ٱمْرَأَ سَوْءٍ وَمَا كَانَتْ أُمُّكِ بَغِيًّا (۲۸)
O sister of Aaron[’s lineage]! Your father was not an evil man, nor was your mother unchaste.’ (28)

Key Points

The word "فَریّا" (fariyyan) means an ugly deed or a great evil (munkar). Although some have considered Harun to be the actual brother of Maryam (Mary), the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Maryam was related to Harun, the brother of Musa (Moses).(32)

32) Tafsir Burhan; Amali al-Murtada, Vol. 4, p. 106.

Messages

1- We must reveal the truth, even if a group becomes upset and levels accusations. «فأتت به قومها تحمله»

2- People's judgment is usually hasty and based on apparent signs, not realities. «لقد جئت شیئاً فریّاً» (Indeed you have brought a monstrous thing.) (Upon seeing the infant with Maryam, they judged her negatively.)

3- One of the methods of upbringing (tarbiyah) is drawing people's attention to the goodness and virtue of their ancestors and family. «یا اُخت هارون...» (O sister of Harun...)

4- Nothing but a righteous child is expected from righteous parents and family. «یا اُخت هارون ماكان ابوك... امّك بغیّاً» (O sister of Harun, your father was not... nor was your mother unchaste.) (The righteousness of the father, mother, and family is effective in the child's behavior and conduct.)

5- Committing an evil deed by someone from a noble lineage is subject to greater reproach. «ماكان ابوك...» (Your father was not...)

Ayahs

فَأَشَارَتْ إِلَيْهِ ۖ قَالُوا۟ كَيْفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَن كَانَ فِى ٱلْمَهْدِ صَبِيًّا (۲۹)
Thereat she pointed to him. They said, ‘How can we speak to one who is yet a baby in the cradle?’ (29)
قَالَ إِنِّى عَبْدُ ٱللَّهِ ءَاتَىٰنِىَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَجَعَلَنِى نَبِيًّا (۳٠)
He said, ‘Indeed I am a servant of Allah! He has given me the Book and made me a prophet. (30)

Key Points

Since Lady Maryam (Mary) had taken a vow of silence (a fast of silence), she gestured instead of speaking, in fulfillment of her vow. «فاشارت» Although the first words of Jesus عليه السلام were about the servitude to Allah, His followers exaggerated (ghuluww) and considered Him to be God and the Son of God! Imam Baqir عليه السلام was asked whether Jesus was also Allah's Proof (Hujjah) over the people while in the cradle? He replied: Jesus عليه السلام was a Prophet (Nabi), but He was not a Messenger (Mursal) until He reached the age of seven, at which point the status of Messengership (Risalah) was also granted to Him. (33) Jesus عليه السلام, with one short sentence, both removed the accusation from His mother, spoke about His own future, and pointed to the future duty of the people. «اِنّى عبداللّه... جعلنى نبیّا»

33) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Where Allah wills, a child speaks in the cradle and silences rumors and seditions (fitnahs). «قال اِنّى عبداللّه...»

2- When introducing ourselves, let us first place the seal of servitude to Allah upon ourselves, for this is the greatest honor. «اِنّى عبداللّه... وجعلنى نبیّا»

3- Servitude to Allah is the source of all Divine blessings (fuyūḍāt). «اِنّى عبداللّه... وجعلنى نبیّا»

4- A specific age is not a condition for Prophethood (and Imamate). «فى المهد... جعلنى نبیّا»

Ayahs

وَجَعَلَنِى مُبَارَكًا أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُ وَأَوْصَـٰنِى بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ مَا دُمْتُ حَيًّا (۳۱)
He has made me blessed, wherever I may be, and He has enjoined me to [maintain] the prayer and to [pay] the zakat as long as I live, (31)
وَبَرًّۢا بِوَٰلِدَتِى وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْنِى جَبَّارًا شَقِيًّا (۳۲)
and to be good to my mother, and He has not made me self-willed and wretched. (32)

Key Points

Something is blessed (mubarak) if it contains abundant benefit, the teaching and disciplining of others, and stability. Prophet Jesus عليه السلام has a blessed life, and He is alive until after the reappearance of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام. Furthermore, His followers are numerous and are victorious over the disbelievers who sought to kill Him. «وجاعل الّذین اتّبعوك فوق الّذین كفروا الى یوم القیامة»(34) The source of goodness (khayrat) and blessings (barakat) lies in intentions, goals, states, and inner characteristics. Therefore, some people are blessed wherever they may be. «جعلنى مباركا أین ما كنت»(35) And others, due to spiritual problems and distance from spirituality, are afflicted by their own improper characteristics wherever they are, and their goodness does not reach others. Jesus عليه السلام, by using the word «والدتى» (my mother), alluded to the chastity of His mother and His lack of a father. «و بَرّا بوالدتى» (and dutiful to my mother) and He did not say: «بوالدىّ» (to my parents).

34) Aal-i-Imran, 55. 35) Maryam, 31.

Messages

1- Know that your privileges and superiorities come from Allah. «جعلنى»

2- Self-praise is permissible, provided it is not for the purpose of boasting. «و جعلنى مباركا...»

3- Prophets are the source of goodness (khayr), blessing (barakah), and eternal effects. «مباركا»

4- Spiritual values transcend time and place. «أین ما كنت... مادمت حیّاً»

5- Prayer (Salat) and Zakat (alms) are common elements among the divine religions. «و اوصانى بالصّلوة والزكوة» (Perhaps the condition for being blessed can be considered the establishment of prayer and the payment of Zakat.)

6- The relationship with Allah (performing prayer) is inseparable from the relationship with the deprived (paying Zakat). «بالصلوة والزكوة»

7- Prayer and Zakat are not subject to suspension throughout one's life. «مادمت حیّاً»

8- Kindness to the mother is among the ethics of the Prophets. «و برّا بوالدتى»

9- Whoever is unkind to his mother will not show mercy to people either. «بَرّاً بوالدتى و لم یجعلنى جباراً شقیّاً»

10- Prophets do not seek tyranny or domination over people. «لم یجعلنى جبّاراً شقیّا»

Ayah

وَٱلسَّلَـٰمُ عَلَىَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدتُّ وَيَوْمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ أُبْعَثُ حَيًّا (۳۳)
Peace to me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised alive.’ (33)

Messages

1- The path of the Prophets is the only path that remains safe from any deviation, from birth until the Resurrection. «والسلام علىّ یوم وُلدتُ و یوم... اُبعث حیّا»

2- In certain instances, self-commendation is permissible. «والسلام علىّ ...»

3- The most important days for a human being are the day of birth, death, and being resurrected on the Day of Judgment.(36) «وُلدتُ... اَموت...اُبعث»

4- Death and the Resurrection are for everyone. (Although Jesus عليه السلام is alive until now and resides in the heavens, He too will die one day.) «یوم اموت»

5- How can you consider someone who is subject to birth, death, and resurrection a partner to Allah? «وُلدتُ... اَموت...اُبعث»

(36) Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The most terrifying days for a human being are three days: the day of birth, the day of death, and the Day of Judgment. Uyoun Akhbar al-Ridha, Vol 1, p 257.

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۚ قَوْلَ ٱلْحَقِّ ٱلَّذِى فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ (۳٤)
That is Jesus, son of Mary, a Word of the Real concerning whom they are in doubt. (34)
مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدٍ ۖ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥٓ ۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (۳٥)
It is not for Allah to take a son. Immaculate is He! When He decides on a matter, He just says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is. (35)

Key Points

In the preceding few verses, Jesus (عليه السلام) was introduced with seven outstanding attributes and two programs. His outstanding attributes are: A: Being a servant of Allah. «اِنّى عبداللّه» B: Bringing the heavenly Book. «آتانى الكتاب» C: Prophethood. «وجعلنى نبیّا» D: Being blessed. «وجعلنى مباركا» E: Kindness to his mother. «و بِرّاً بوالدتى» F: Not being tyrannical or wretched. «لم یجعلنى جبّاراً شقیّاً» G: Being humble, grateful (recognizing the truth), and felicitous. «والسّلام علىّ...» As for the two programs of Jesus, one is prayer (Salat) and the other is charity (Zakat).

This verse states: The correct statement regarding Jesus is what We have said. «ذلك عیسى بن مریم» Allah has repeatedly deemed Christians deviated and their beliefs concerning Prophet Jesus invalid in the Quran, just as He criticized them in Ayah 73 of Surah Al-Ma'idah: «لقد كفر الّذین قالوا اِنّ اللّه ثالث ثلاثة» and Ayah 30 of Surah At-Tawbah: «وقالت النّصارى المسیح ابن اللّه». However, here, by explaining the true portrait of Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام), He shows the correct path opposite the false path.

For the realization of Allah's Will, no utterance, not even the word «كُن» (Be), is necessary, but Allah has expressed His Will in this form for our understanding. Allah is the Absolute Omnipotent and needs no means (causes) in creation. His infinite power is proof that He is far removed (transcendent) from helplessness, inability, and the adoption of a son.

The Quran and Christ

Following intense pressure from the disbelievers of Mecca upon the followers of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله), a group of Muslims, accompanied by Ja'far ibn Abi Talib (عليهما السلام), migrated to Abyssinia (Ethiopia). The Quraysh disbelievers said: If the Muslims gain power there and establish a government, they will eradicate our idol worship. They sent 'Amr ibn al-'As and a group, along with gifts for the ministers of Negus (Najashi), to Abyssinia. They accepted the gifts in order to incite Negus against the immigrant Muslims; but Negus decided to personally summon the Muslims and hear their words.

Ja'far, who was the spokesman for the Muslims, began his speech in the presence of the King of Abyssinia. He spoke so eloquently about the darkness of idol worship, superstitions, and aggressions during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), and about the dawn of Islam and the luminosity of the religion of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله), that Negus wept, returned the gifts of the disbelievers, and said: Allah, Who gave me power, did not take a bribe from me, so why should I take a bribe from you?! This session ended in favor of the Muslims and to the detriment of the disbelievers.

Following this defeat, 'Amr ibn al-'As fell into thought and proposed a new idea the next day. In order to provoke Negus's religious zeal, he said: The Muslims hold beliefs that are contrary to your belief. Negus once again summoned the Muslims and inquired about their belief concerning Prophet Christ (عليه السلام). Ja'far al-Tayyar, in response, recited verses from Surah Maryam up to the verse: «ذلك عیسى بن مریم قول الحقّ الّذى فیه یمترون». Upon hearing the luminous verses of the Quran, Negus shed tears and said: This is the truth. When 'Amr ibn al-'As, after his renewed defeat and disgrace, attempted to make another move, Negus raised His hand and struck him a hard slap.(37)

37) Furugh-e Abadiyyat (Eternal Radiance), Vol. 1, p. 253, quoting Kamil Ibn Athir, Vol. 2, p. 54; Bihar, Vol. 18, p. 415.

Messages

1- In the face of deviants and deviant beliefs, showing the correct path is necessary. «ذلك عیسى بن مریم قول الحقّ»

2- The dignity of Allah is incompatible with having a son. «ماكان للّه ...» (Because the desire for a son is either due to our limited lifespan, or due to our need for human resources, or because of human spiritual and emotional needs, all of which Allah is transcendent from. «سبحانه»)

3- Allah has no son whatsoever. «من ولد» (Contrary to some who imagined the angels to be the daughters of God, or Jesus to be the son of God.)

4- A son must be of the same nature as the father and like the father, but Allah has no likeness. «سبحانه»

5- Whenever we quote the inappropriate statements of others, we should immediately clear our own minds and the minds of others with a monotheistic phrase. «سبحانه»

6- Not only were Jesus (عليه السلام) created without a father and Adam (عليه السلام) created without a father and mother, but everything is brought into existence by the Will of Allah. «كن فیكون»

7- There is no gap between Allah's Will and the realization of things. (Indicated by) the letter 'fa' in «فیكون».

8- The relationship between creatures and Allah is the relationship between the Creator and the created, not the relationship between father and son. «كن فیكون»

Ayah

وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى وَرَبُّكُمْ فَٱعْبُدُوهُ ۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (۳٦)
[And Jesus said,] ‘Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him. This is a straight path.’ (36)

Key Points

Jesus عليه السلام said in introducing Himself: I am the servant of Allah, "انّى عبداللّه". The first command He received was concerning prayer and worship, "و اوصانى بالصلوة". And the first program He announced was the worship and servitude of Allah. "فاعبدوه". The insistence of Christ upon monotheism and His emphasis that the straight path is only this and nothing else, is a response to the proponents of the Trinity and others. (38)

(38) A verse similar to this one is also found in Surah Aal-i-Imran, Ayah 51, and Surah Az-Zukhruf, Ayah 64.

Messages

1- Worship is deserving of the One who is our Lord. «ربّى و ربّكم فاعبدوه»

2- Considering Jesus the son of God is a distortion of religion and a cause of disbelief (Kufr). «اللّه ربّى و ربّكم فاعبدوه»

3- Monotheism (Tawhid) and servitude to Allah is the straight path, and all other paths are deviations. (In the Quran, the path of Allah, the Messenger, worship, and holding fast to Allah are introduced as the straight path.) «هذا صراط مستقيم»

Ayahs

فَٱخْتَلَفَ ٱلْأَحْزَابُ مِنۢ بَيْنِهِمْ ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن مَّشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۳۷)
But the factions differed among themselves. So woe to the faithless at the scene of a tremendous day. (37)
أَسْمِعْ بِهِمْ وَأَبْصِرْ يَوْمَ يَأْتُونَنَا ۖ لَـٰكِنِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ ٱلْيَوْمَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳۸)
How well they will hear and how well they will see on the day when they come to Us! But today the wrongdoers are in manifest error. (38)

Key Points

The word Hizb (party/group) refers to a group that possesses specific and unified positions and goals. The word Mashhad (scene/witnessing) means either the place where people are present, or the place of witnessing and giving testimony. This is because on the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah), people will be present, and many witnesses, including angels and prophets, will testify regarding human deeds. Some people considered Jesus (عليه السلام) to be God, such as the Ya'qubiyyah (Jacobite) sect, and some said he was the Son of God, like the Nusturiyyah (Nestorian) sect, and a group believed in the Trinity, such as the Isra'iliyyah (Israelite) sect, but all of them will testify on the Day of Resurrection to the falsehood of their beliefs.(39)

39) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Despite the fact that Jesus (عليه السلام) showed the straight path, a group still created division. «فاختَلَف»

2- Partisanship (Hizb-oriented thinking) and conscious disagreement have existed throughout history. «فاختَلف الاحزاب»

3- The disbelievers are oppressors (they commit oppression against themselves, their doctrine, and society). «للّذین كفروا... الظالمون»

Ayahs

وَأَنذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْحَسْرَةِ إِذْ قُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ وَهُمْ فِى غَفْلَةٍ وَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۳۹)
Warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be decided, while they are [yet] heedless and do not have faith. (39)
إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَرِثُ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْهَا وَإِلَيْنَا يُرْجَعُونَ (٤٠)
Indeed We shall inherit the earth and whoever there is on it, and to Us they shall be brought back. (40)

Key Points

The key to all misfortunes is heedlessness. Heedlessness of Allah, heedlessness of the Resurrection (Ma'ad), heedlessness of the effects and consequences of sin, heedlessness of conspiracies, heedlessness of the deprived, heedlessness of history and its traditions, and heedlessness of youth, talents, and opportunities for growth. Similar to this Ayah, instances are found in the Qur'an, including: A: «كلّ مَن علیها فان و یَبقى وجه ربّك ذوالجلال و الاكرام»(40) All people perish, and only the Essence of Allah, the Possessor of Majesty and Bounty, remains. B: «وللّه میراث السموات والارض»(41) The inheritance of the heavens and the earth belongs solely to Allah. One of the names of the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah) is "Yawm al-Hasrah" (The Day of Regret/Sorrow). Regret over lost opportunities and wasted assets.

(40) Ar-Rahman, 26-27. (41) Al-Hadid, 10.

Messages

1- One of the duties of the Prophets is warning (Inthar). «وانذرهم»

2- Do whatever you can in this world, for on the Day of Resurrection, the matter will be settled (it will be too late). «قضى الامر»

3- Heedlessness is the ground for disbelief (Kufr). «فى غفلة و هم لایؤمنون»

4- Dying in a state of heedlessness and lack of faith causes regret. «فى غفلة و هم لایؤمنون»

5- Possessions lead to heedlessness, and heedlessness leads to disbelief. «فى غفلة و هم لایؤمنون انا نحن نرث الارض»

Ayah

وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقًا نَّبِيًّا (٤۱)
And mention in the Book Abraham. Indeed, he was a truthful man and a prophet. (41)

Key Points

The word Ṣiddīq (صِدّیق) is applied both to someone who greatly affirms the truth (al-Haqq) and to someone whose entire speech and conduct are based on truthfulness (sincerity). Whatever he says, he acts upon, and whatever he acts upon, he says.

Messages

1- We must keep the memory of the men of Allah alive. «واذكُر»

2- Paying attention to the perfections of others and honoring the great figures is one of the methods of education (upbringing). «واذكر»

3- Truthfulness is a condition for prophethood. «صدیّقاً نبیّا»

4- Truthfulness is valuable when it is part of a person's character and disposition. «كان صدیّقاً»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ لِمَ تَعْبُدُ مَا لَا يَسْمَعُ وَلَا يُبْصِرُ وَلَا يُغْنِى عَنكَ شَيْـًٔا (٤۲)
When he said to his father, ‘Father! Why do you worship that which neither hears nor sees, and is of no avail to you in any way? (42)

Key Points

The father of Prophet Ibrahim had passed away, and the person introduced in the Quran as his father was his guardian, either his uncle Azar or his mother's husband. (42) We also read in the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام that the father of Prophet Ibrahim was a monotheist (muwahhid), and the meaning of "أب" (ab) here is his uncle. In the Arabic language, the word "أب" (ab) has a broad meaning and is also applied to a teacher (mu'allim), an educator (murabbi), and even a father-in-law. It is also narrated in traditions from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that He said: «أنا و علىّ اَبَوا هذه الاُمّة» (I and Ali are the fathers of this Ummah). (43)

42) Tafsir al-Mizan. 43) Bihar, Vol. 16, p. 95.

Messages

1- The debates of Ibrahim with his uncle are worthy of remembrance and commemoration. «اذ قال»

2- In forbidding the evil (nahy az munkar), we start with our close relatives. «یا اَبتِ»

3- Forbidding the evil has no age limit. (A son can forbid the head of the family, but he must maintain respect for them.) «یا اَبتِ»

4- The perfection of individuals is not always dependent on their age; sometimes the child of the house understands better than the elders. «یا ابت»

5- Guidance and propagation should not always be general; sometimes they must be addressed to the individual person. «یا أَبَتِ»

6- In forbidding the evil, we bring the conscience of individuals to judgment by posing a question. «لم تعبد»

7- In forbidding the evil, we should start with ideological evils. (Unfortunately, we are mostly preoccupied with moral and social evils.) «لم تعبد»

8- We should not sacrifice the path of truth for emotions. «لابیه لم تعبد» Kinship should not prevent forbidding the evil.

9- Idol worship is worse than angel worship, human worship, or cow worship, because in those (latter forms), there is sight, hearing, and consciousness, but not in the idol. «مالا یسمع ...»

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّى قَدْ جَآءَنِى مِنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ مَا لَمْ يَأْتِكَ فَٱتَّبِعْنِىٓ أَهْدِكَ صِرَٰطًا سَوِيًّا (٤۳)
Father! Indeed a knowledge has already come to me, which has not come to you. So follow me that I may guide you to a right path. (43)
يَـٰٓأَبَتِ لَا تَعْبُدِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ كَانَ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ عَصِيًّا (٤٤)
Father! Do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan is disobedient to the All-beneficent. (44)

Key Points

The intent behind the worship of Satan is simply obedience and adherence to him, which renders man a servant and slave of Satan.

Messages

1- Knowledge entails responsibility. «جائنى من العلم», «فاتّبعنى»

2- The knowledge of the prophets is also limited. «من العلم»

3- The one who forbids evil must himself be knowledgeable. «جائنى من العلم... لاتعبد...»

4- In certain instances, declaring one's merit is necessary. «قد جاءنى من العلم مالم یأتك»

5- The root of polytheism (shirk) is ignorance. «جاءنى من العلم مالم یأتك»

6- People must follow the knowledgeable. «جاءنى من العلم ... فاتّبعنى»

7- A young individual can be the leader (Imam) of adults. «فاتّبعنى»

8- Following the prophets means being placed upon the straight path. «فاتّبعنى اهدك صراطاً سویّا»

9- The path of the prophets is the path of moderation, neither extremism nor negligence. «صراطاً سویّا»

10- When forbidding evil, we should also state the reason. «لا تعبد الشیطان... كان للرّحمن عَصیّا»

11- The guardianship (wilayah) and friendship of Satan are dangerous. If he disobeyed God, what will he bring upon us? «اِنّ الشیطان كان للرّحمن عَصیّا» (How heinous is the disobedience of the All-Merciful, Who is the source of all mercies?)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ أَن يَمَسَّكَ عَذَابٌ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّيْطَـٰنِ وَلِيًّا (٤٥)
Father! I am indeed afraid that a punishment from the All-beneficent will befall you, and you will become Satan’s accomplice.’ (45)

Messages

1- In Dawah and propagation, we should prepare the ground for acceptance by using emotions. (In these verses, "یا ابت" [O my father] has appeared four times.)

2- The Prophets are compassionate towards the people. «اِنّى اخاف»

3- Man reaches a point where he provokes the source of Mercy to wrath and angers Allah, the Most Compassionate (al-Rahman). «عذاب من الرّحمن»

4- If the relatives of the Prophet are also deviated, they will be afflicted by divine punishment. «یا ابت انّى اخاف... عذاب»

5- Whoever does not remain on the straight path will fall under the guardianship (Wilayah) of Satan. «اهدك صراطاً سویّا ... فتكون للشیطان ولیّا»

6- If we love someone, we must not love his enemy. «للرّحمن عصیّا... للشیطان ولیّا»

Ayah

قَالَ أَرَاغِبٌ أَنتَ عَنْ ءَالِهَتِى يَـٰٓإِبْرَٰهِيمُ ۖ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ لَأَرْجُمَنَّكَ ۖ وَٱهْجُرْنِى مَلِيًّا (٤٦)
He said, ‘Abraham! Are you renouncing my gods? If you do not desist, I will stone you. Get away from me for a long while.’ (46)

Key Points

The word «مَلیّاً» (maliyyā) is derived from «اِملاء» (imlā'), meaning a long period of respite.

Messages

1- Sometimes one must conceal their beliefs. (It seems that until that moment, Ibrahim had concealed his beliefs even from his uncle, because his uncle spoke to him with surprise.) «أراغب»

2- This Ayah is a source of comfort for the Prophet: if Abu Lahab, your uncle, is an infidel and threatens you, the uncle of Ibrahim was also like that. «قال أراغبٌ أنت»

3- Man reaches a point where the word of truth is bitter for him and causes surprise. «أراغبٌ أنت»

4- Refusing to accept the word of truth from someone younger than oneself is a sign of arrogance. «أراغبٌ أنت»

5- Whoever does not accept one conscious God will accept several unconscious gods. «آلِهتى»

6- Speaking the truth and fighting against false traditions leads to displacement and deprivation. «لارجمنّك واهجرنى مَلیّا»

Ayah

قَالَ سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكَ ۖ سَأَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّىٓ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِى حَفِيًّا (٤۷)
He said, ‘Peace be to you! I shall plead with my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, He is gracious to me. (47)

Key Points

The word "حَفىّ" (Hafiyy) is used for someone who shows perfect kindness and respect toward another (44), and sometimes it means knowledgeable (or wise). In these verses, the dispositions of the believer and the disbeliever are clearly visible in their dialogue:

A: Abraham speaks affectionately, while his uncle speaks harshly. Abraham says "یا أبتِ" (O my father!) four times, but his idol-worshipping uncle did not even once say "یا بنىّ" (O my son!). B: Abraham speaks with reasoning, but Azar speaks without evidence. Abraham says: "لِمَ تعبد ما لا یسمع" (Why do you worship that which hears not?), while his uncle says: "آلهتى" (My gods). C: Abraham speaks compassionately, but Azar replies with a threat. D: Abraham offers peace (Salam), but Azar commands him to keep away. "واهجرنى" (And forsake me).

Be not less than the shade-giving tree; whoever throws a stone at you, grant him fruit.

The seeking of forgiveness (Istighfar) by Prophet Abraham for his idol-worshipping uncle was because he held out the possibility of his guidance. "سأستغفرلك" (I shall ask forgiveness for you). But as soon as he despaired of his uncle's guidance, he disassociated himself from him. The Quran states: "وما كان استغفار ابراهیم لابیه الاّ عن موعدة وعدها ایّاه فلمّا تبین له انّه عدوّ للّه تبرّء منه" (45)

44) Lisan al-Arab. 45) At-Tawbah, 114.

Messages

1- Men of God are patient and large-hearted in the face of threats and humiliation from others. "قال سلامٌ علیك" (He said: Peace be upon you).

2- We should try to extinguish the fire of others' anger with beautiful and calming words. "لارجمنّك ... قال سلامٌ علیك" (I will surely stone you... He said: Peace be upon you).

3- The seeking of forgiveness (Istighfar) by the Prophets and the Awliya (saints) has special effects. (Otherwise, the promise of Istighfar would not play a significant role.) "ساستغفر لك" (I shall ask forgiveness for you).

4- Accepting apologies is among the attributes of Lordship (Rububiyyah). "ساستغفر لك ربّى" (I shall ask forgiveness for you from my Lord).

5- Specific times have priority for seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). "ساستغفر" (I shall ask forgiveness) means I will pray at the appropriate time.

6- Among the etiquette of supplication (Du'a) is paying attention to the Lordship of Allah. "ربّى" (My Lord).

7- In the face of opponents' threats, we should remember the favors of Allah. "انّه كان بى حفیّا" (Indeed, He has been gracious to me).

Ayah

وَأَعْتَزِلُكُمْ وَمَا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَدْعُوا۟ رَبِّى عَسَىٰٓ أَلَّآ أَكُونَ بِدُعَآءِ رَبِّى شَقِيًّا (٤۸)
I dissociate myself from you and whatever you invoke besides Allah. I will supplicate my Lord. Hopefully, I will not be disappointed in supplicating my Lord.’ (48)

Key Points

God-worshipping youth who live in deviant families must follow the path of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham). Invitation accompanied by respect/manners, "یا أبت" (O my father); compassion and accompanied by logic, "لِمَ تعبد..." (Why do you worship...); warning accompanied by supplication, "انّى اخاف... ساستغفر لك" (Indeed, I fear... I will ask forgiveness for you); peace accompanied by seeking forgiveness, "سلام علیك ساستغفر لك" (Peace be upon you, I will ask forgiveness for you); and finally, separation. "واعتزلكم" (I will separate myself from you).

Messages

1- One of the final stages of forbidding evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar) is cutting off relations. "واعتزلكم" (If we cannot change a corrupt environment, we should at least leave it.)

2- One of the fundamental principles of Divine schools of thought is dissociation (Bara'ah) from the disbelievers. "واعتزلكم"

3- Dissociation (Bara'ah) precedes allegiance (Wilayah). First, we must separate ourselves from polytheism (Shirk), tyranny (Taghut), and corruption, and then connect ourselves to the Truth. "اعتزلكم... ادعوا ربّى"

Yes, when the demon goes out, the angel comes in. First "لا اله" (there is no god), then "الاّ اللّه" (except Allah). In the Quran, we first read the phrase "یكفر بالطاغوت" (he disbelieves in the Taghut) and then the phrase "یؤمن باللّه" (he believes in Allah) (46).

4- If we want our supplication (Du'a) to become an Abrahamic supplication, we must distance ourselves from polluted individuals and centers. "اعتزلكم... ادعوا"

5- The word "ربّ" (Lord) plays an important role in supplication. "ادعوا ربّى - بدعاء ربّى" (I will call upon my Lord - by the supplication of my Lord).

6- The believer is between fear and hope (Raja'). "عسى..." (Perhaps...). If fear dominates a person, it leads him to despair, and if hope dominates him, it makes him self-indulgent. Therefore, a person must be between fear and hope, and apprehension and expectation.

7- The answering of supplication is a Divine favor. "عسى" (Perhaps).

8- Even the Prophets are hopeful in Allah regarding their future (47). "عسى الا اكون... شقیّا" (Perhaps I will not be... wretched).

46) Baqarah, 256.

47) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا ٱعْتَزَلَهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۖ وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنَا نَبِيًّا (٤۹)
So when he had left them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each We made a prophet. (49)
وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِنَا وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا (٥٠)
And We gave them out of Our mercy, and conferred on them a worthy and lofty repute. (50)

Key Points

Portrait of Ibrahim عليه السلام * Ibrahim عليه السلام detached his heart from the stars, the moon, and the sun, and reached Allah. «لا احبّ الافلین... وجّهت وجهى...»(48) * He abandoned his misguided uncle, and himself became a father to all people. «ملّة ابیكم»(49) * His uncle shunned him, «واهجرنى ملیّاً»(50) but Allah showed Him kindness. «انّه كان بى حفیاً»(51) * He withdrew for the sake of Allah, «اعتزلكم»(52) and became famous throughout the world. «جعلنالهم لسان صدق» * He fled from the house of idols, and became the builder of the House of Monotheism. «اذیرفع ابراهیم القواعدمن البیت»(53) * He surrendered his life in the way of Allah, and the fire became a garden for him. «یاناركونى برداًوسلاماً»(54) * He prepared his son for sacrifice, «و تلّه للجبین»(55) saved his life, and the tradition of sacrifice became obligatory in its place. * He was childless until late in life, yet content; at the end of his life, prophethood was placed in his lineage. * He built the Ka'bah in the most secluded of places, and it became the most attractive region on Earth. * He cried out, all alone, in the desert, and those who answered the call (Labbayk) arrived from all over the Earth. «اذّن فى النّاس بالحجّ»(56) * He left his thirsty child, and the water of Zamzam flowed forever. * He offered peace (Salam) in the face of the threat from his idol-worshipping uncle, «قال سلامٌ علیك»(57) and Allah sent peace upon him. «سلامٌ على ابراهیم»(58) * He was one man, yet today all heavenly schools of thought consider themselves Abrahamic. * Everyone showed hostility towards him, but Allah considered him His intimate friend (Khalil). «واتّخذاللّه ابراهیم خلیلا»(59)

Messages

1- We must separate ourselves both from the sinner and from the sin, because withdrawal from the misguided is effective only when accompanied by withdrawal from the misguidance itself. «اعتزلكم و ماتدعون - اعتزلهم و ما یعبدون»

2- A righteous child is the reward for the struggles (Mujahadat) of the parents. «فلمّا اعتزلهم ... وهبنا»

3- If we abandon what is other than Allah, Divine aid will arrive. «فلمّا اعتزلهم... وهبنا»

4- More important than the child is his spiritual status. «وهبنا له اسحق و یعقوب و كلاً جعلنا نبیاً»

5- A person's grandchild is also among the Divine favors. «وهبنا له اسحق و یعقوب» (Ya'qub was the grandson of Ibrahim.)(60)

6- Prophethood is a status determined by Allah. «جعلنا نبیّاً»

7- A single Divine action by a grandfather can immerse a generation in Divine Mercy. «اعتزلهم... وهبنا له»

8- Whatever anyone possesses is from Allah. «وهبنا... جعلنا... رحمتنا»

9- The Mercy of Allah is so vast that whatever the Prophets receive is only a fraction of it. «من رحمتنا»

10- Popularity and good reputation are among the Divine favors. «جعلنا لهم لسان صدق» (The status of Ibrahim reached a point where all polytheists, Jews, and Christians claimed: Ibrahim belongs to us.)

11- The prayer of the men of Allah is answered. (Ibrahim asked Allah for a good name: «واجعل لى لسان صدق فى الاخرین» and in this Ayah, He says: «جعلنا لهم لسان صدق علیّا»60) فبشّرناها باسحق و من وراء اسحق یعقوب» هود، 71.

Ayah

وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مُوسَىٰٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مُخْلَصًا وَكَانَ رَسُولًا نَّبِيًّا (٥۱)
And mention in the Book Moses. Indeed he was exclusively dedicated [to Allah], and an apostle and prophet. (51)

Key Points

The word mukhliṣ (with a kasrah under the lām) means someone who works only for Allah, but Satan might influence him and lead him to polytheism (shirk). How many are those who are sincere (mukhliṣ) during punishment or drowning, but revert to polytheism after being saved. However, the word mukhlaṣ refers to that group of chosen individuals whom Satan has no way of influencing. «الاّ عبادك منهم المخلَصین»(61) According to Tafsir Kashshāf, mukhliṣ is someone who works for God, and mukhlaṣ is someone whom Allah has purified and chosen for Himself, and nothing other than God affects him. We see the selection of Moses in other verses: «و اَنَا اختَرتُك»(62) I have chosen you, and in another place it states: «واصطنعَتك لنفسى»(63) I have chosen you for Myself. Prophet Moses عليه السلام was both a Messenger (Rasūl) and a Prophet (Nabī). A Nabī is someone who hears the voice of the angel but does not see the angel himself, whereas a Rasūl, in addition to hearing the voice, also sees the angel himself.(64) «رسولاً نبیّاً»

Messages

1- Remembering the great figures has educational and exemplary effects. «واذكر...» Familiarity with the history of the Prophets and their struggles, the divine traditions, and unseen aids provides a basis for gaining experience and lessons, and is a source of encouragement. (Especially the eventful history of Moses, which is dedicated hundreds of verses). «واذكر»

2- Spiritual values are the criterion for commemoration. «واذكر... انه كان مخلصا»

3- Sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) is the basis for attaining spiritual ranks. «مخلصا... رسولا نبیّا»

4- The Prophets are infallible, and Satan has no influence over them. «مخلَصاً»

5- Allah has purified individuals for the completion of the message (65). «كان مخلَصاً»(65) As we also read in Ayah 45 of Sūrah Ṣād: «انّا اخلصناهم»

Ayah

وَنَـٰدَيْنَـٰهُ مِن جَانِبِ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلْأَيْمَنِ وَقَرَّبْنَـٰهُ نَجِيًّا (٥۲)
We called him from the right side of the Mount and We drew him near [to Ourselves] for confidential discourse. (52)

Key Points

The word "Ayman" (أیمن) is either derived from "Yumn" (یُمن), meaning blessed, or from "Yamin" (یَمین), meaning the right side. At the beginning of the Ayah, a call (Nida) is mentioned, and at its end, a private conversation (Munajat) is presented. This is a sign of Allah's kindness, like a noble master calling his servant loudly, saying, "Come sit near Me so we can speak privately." (66) In the Quran, the conversation and communication of Moses (عليه السلام) with Allah are referred to by "Takallum" (speech), "كلّم اللّه موسى تكلیما" (67), and also by "Nida" (call), "نادیناه," and by "Najwa" (secret counsel). "قرّبناه نجیّا" The meaning of proximity (Qurb) in the Ayah is not physical proximity or closeness of location, but rather proximity of status (Qurb Maqami). Mount Tur (Sinai) is located between Egypt and Midian. (68)

Messages

1- Almighty Allah, through His grace and kindness, gradually brings the Prophets closer to Him. "نادیناه... قرّبناه"

2- Some places possess sanctity. "جانب الطور"

3- The station of proximity (Qurb) and secret counsel (Najwa) is the highest of stations. (69) "و قرّبناه نجیّا"

4- Allah speaks secretly and confidentially about certain matters with the Prophets. "نجیّا"

69) Imam Ali (عليه السلام) says: "اذا اشتغل اهل الجنة بالجنة اشتغل اهل اللّه باللّه" When the people of Paradise are occupied with Paradise, the people of Allah are occupied with Allah (and engage in intimate conversation and supplication with Him).

Ayah

وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥ مِن رَّحْمَتِنَآ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ نَبِيًّا (٥۳)
And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron, a prophet. (53)

Messages

1- The prayer of the prophets is answered. Moses (عليه السلام) asked Allah to make his brother Aaron His minister. «واجعل لى وزیراً من اهلى . هارون اخى . اشدد به ازرى»(70) Allah also agreed to his request. «وهبنا له... هارون نبیّاً»

2- A good colleague is one of the Divine favors. «و وهبنا له من رحمتنا... هارون نبیّا»

3- Multiple prophets may be present at the same time. «و وهبنا له من رحمتنا... هارون نبیّا»

4- The status of prophethood does not prevent one prophet from following another prophet. «وهبناله... هارون نبیّا»70) Taha, 29-31.

Ayahs

وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صَادِقَ ٱلْوَعْدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولًا نَّبِيًّا (٥٤)
And mention in the Book Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and an apostle and prophet. (54)
وَكَانَ يَأْمُرُ أَهْلَهُۥ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ وَكَانَ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦ مَرْضِيًّا (٥٥)
He used to bid his family to [maintain] the prayer and to [pay] the zakat, and was pleasing to his Lord. (55)

Key Points

The intent behind Isma'il is either the son of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) or another prophet from the Children of Israel named Isma'il ibn Hizqil. All prophets were truthful in their promise (sadiq al-wa'd), but the manifestation of this trait was more pronounced in Isma'il عليه السلام. It is narrated concerning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that He had an appointment with a person near the Ka'bah, and that person forgot. The Prophet waited for three days until the person was informed; the person then came and apologized.(71) Likewise, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله made an appointment with someone to meet him at noon, but since he did not come, the Prophet waited until sunset. Being truthful in promise is a Divine attribute: «ان اللّه لایخلف المیعاد» (Indeed, Allah does not break the promise)(72). Breaking a promise is a sign of hypocrisy (nifaq). We read in the narrations: The sign of a hypocrite is three things: betrayal of trust, lying in speech, and breaking a promise.(73) The station of pleasure (ridwan) and satisfaction is the greatest desire and goal of the prophets. «واجعله رب رضیا» (And make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You])(74), «وكان عند ربه مرضیّا» (And he was, in the sight of his Lord, pleasing)(75). We read in a narration: When the people of Paradise are settled therein, a call comes: Do you have any other need? They say: «ربّنا رضاك» (Our Lord! Your pleasure is our goal).(76)

Messages

1- Honoring great figures is necessary. «واذكر فى الكتاب اسماعیل»

2- Honoring great figures must be lasting and profound, not superficial and fleeting. «فى الكتاب»

3- The criterion for honoring must be lasting spiritual perfections. «كان صادق الوعد»

4- Fulfilling a covenant (promise) is one of the valuable human attributes. «كان صادق الوعد»

5- Prophets, besides their social responsibilities, also have family responsibilities. «و كان یأمر اهله»

6- Enjoining prayer (salat) and charity (zakat) is among the duties of the father of the family. «وكان یأمر اهله بالصلوة و الزكوة» (Although enjoining prayer is present to some extent in families, enjoining zakat is not.)

7- Enjoining prayer and zakat are instances of enjoining good (amr bi al-ma'ruf). «وكان یأمر»

8- Prayer and zakat have a very long history. «وكان یأمر ...»

9- Prayer (connection with the Creator) and zakat (connection with the creation) are not separate. (Physical worship alone is not a condition for perfection.) «بالصلوة والزكوة»

10- In enjoining good and recommending prayer, we must start with our close relatives. «كان یأمراهله»

11- Guidance and recommendation regarding prayer must be continuous. «وكان یأمر ...»

Ayahs

وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ إِدْرِيسَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقًا نَّبِيًّا (٥٦)
And mention in the Book Idrees. Indeed, he was a truthful one and a prophet, (56)
وَرَفَعْنَـٰهُ مَكَانًا عَلِيًّا (٥۷)
and We raised him to an exalted station. (57)

Key Points

The meaning of «مكاناً علیّا» (a high place) is either a high spiritual station or ascension to the heavens, because Allah, besides the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, took four other prophets to the heavens: Idris, Isa (Jesus), Khidr, and Ilyas (Elias). (77) A «صدّیق» (Truthful One) is someone whose words and actions are in harmony with each other.

Portrait of Idris علیه السلام

  • Prophet Idris was among the ancestors of Prophet Nuh (Noah). He became known as Idris either due to His extensive engagement in study (78) or because He was the first person to write with a pen. (79)
  • The noble name of Idris is mentioned twice in the Quran, and He is honored with the descriptions of «صدّیق» (Truthful), «صابر» (Patient), and «نبىّ» (Prophet).
  • In a hadith, we read: The house of Idris was located in Masjid al-Sahla, near Najaf al-Ashraf. (80)
  • Idris was the first person who knew the science of astronomy and calculation. (81)
  • He was the first person who taught tailoring to mankind. (82) *Idris علیه السلام lived for 365 years, then ascended to the heavens, and He is currently alive and will be present during the reappearance of Imam Mahdi علیه السلام. (83)

Messages

1- Remembering and honoring great figures is a means for the growth and guidance of others. «واذكر» (And mention)

2- Honors must remain enduring. «فى الكتاب» (in the Book)

3- The criterion for honoring individuals must be their spiritual values. «صدّیقاً نبیّاً» (a Truthful One, a Prophet)

4- Sincerity (Truthfulness) is a condition for effectiveness in propagation (Da'wah). «صدّیقاً نبیّاً» The precedence of the word «صدّیق» over «نبىّ» indicates that sincerity is the foundation of prophethood.

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ مِن ذُرِّيَّةِ ءَادَمَ وَمِمَّنْ حَمَلْنَا مَعَ نُوحٍ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَمِمَّنْ هَدَيْنَا وَٱجْتَبَيْنَآ ۚ إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ خَرُّوا۟ سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا ۩ (٥۸)
They are the ones whom Allah has blessed from among the prophets of Adam’s progeny, and from [the progeny of] those We carried with Noah, and from among the progeny of Abraham and Israel, and from among those that We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-beneficent were recited to them, they would fall down weeping in prostration. (58)

Key Points

The names of ten prophets were mentioned in the preceding verses: Zachariah, John (Yahya), Jesus (Isa), Abraham (Ibrahim), Isaac (Ishaq), Jacob (Ya'qub), Moses (Musa), Aaron (Harun), Ishmael (Isma'il), and Idris. Now, this verse, referring to them, states: They are those whom Allah bestowed favor upon. This group consists of those whose path we ask Allah to guide us to ten times every day in our prayers, not the path of the misguided and deviated. "اهدنا الصراط المستقیم . صراط الّذین انعمت علیهم"

The intent of "the progeny of Adam" is Idris, and the intent of "ذّریّة مَن حمَلنا مع نوح" (the progeny of those We carried with Noah) is Prophet Abraham, who is the grandson of Noah. The intent of "the progeny of Abraham" is Isaac, Ishmael, and Jacob, and the intent of "the progeny of Israel" is Moses, Aaron, Zachariah, John, and Jesus. (84)

We read in a Hadith: When reciting the Quran, become sorrowful and weep, or put yourselves in a state of weeping. (85)

The best examples of the Divine chosen ones who performed lengthy prostrations accompanied by tears were the Prophet of Islam and his infallible Household (Ahl al-Bayt) علیهم السلام. Regarding all of them, there are accounts of their numerous prostrations and weeping during the supplications (Munajat) of the month of Ramadan, Arafah, and so on.

84) Tafsir Namuneh. 85) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The goal of all the prophets is one. (Allah places the prophets side by side and describes them all with a single attribute) "اولئك الّذین انعم اللّه علیهم ..."

2- The true Bestower of Favor is Allah. "انعم اللّه علیهم"

3- Parents and ancestors are effective in the upbringing of the next generation through heredity. "ذرّیةآدم... ذرّیة ابراهیم"

4- Although all prophets are from the progeny of Adam, mentioning Noah, Abraham, and Israel (Jacob) specifically honors them particularly. "مع نوح و من ذرّیة ..."

5- Weeping during prostration is so valuable that Allah has honored those who practice it. "سجّداً و بكیّا"

6- Weeping and lamenting before the Divine Presence is a sign of growth, recognition, success, and being chosen. "و ممنّ هدینا واجتبینا..."

Ayahs

۞ فَخَلَفَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ أَضَاعُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَوَٰتِ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا (٥۹)
But they were succeeded by an evil posterity who neglected the prayer, and followed [their base] appetites. So they will soon encounter [the reward of] perversity, (59)
إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ شَيْـًٔا (٦٠)
barring those who repent, believe, and act righteously. Such will enter paradise, and they will not be wronged in the least. (60)

Key Points

KhalaF (with a fathah on the lām) refers to a righteous offspring, while Khalf (with a sukūn on the lām) is an unrighteous offspring. The word ghayy is the opposite of rushd (guidance/maturity). «قد تبیّن الرّشد من الغىّ» (86) Wasting the prayer (iḍā'at al-ṣalāh) is different from not performing it or abandoning it. A person who performs the prayer but without observing its conditions or with delay has wasted the prayer and treated it lightly. (87) We read in a narration that as soon as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله recited this Ayah, He became deeply affected and said: After 60 years, a group will waste the prayer. (88) If this statement was made in the early part of the first year of the Hijra, the year 60 is the same year as the martyrdom of Imam Husayn عليه السلام and the year Yazīd came to power.

Repentance (Tawbah) in the Quran

  • Usually, following the verses of punishment, the Holy Quran includes the phrase «الاّالّذین تابوا» (except those who repent) or «الاّ من تاب» (except whoever repents) to indicate that the path to reform is never closed to anyone.
  • Repentance is obligatory, because it is the command of Allah. «توبوا الى اللّه» (89)
  • The acceptance of genuine repentance is certain, because it is unbelievable that we would repent by His command and yet He would not accept it. «هوالّذى یقبل التوبة عن عباده» (90), «هوالتّواب الرحیم» (91)
  • Allah both accepts repentance and loves those who repent frequently. «یحبّ التّوابین» (92)
  • Repentance must be accompanied by good deeds and compensation for sins. «تاب و عمل صالحا» (93), «تاب من بعده و اصلح» (94), «تابوا واصلحوا و بیّنوا» (95)
  • Repentance is the key to salvation. «توبوا ... لعلكم تفلحون» (96)
  • Repentance is the means by which evil deeds are transformed into good deeds. «الاّ من تاب و عمل صالحاً فاولئك یبدّل اللّه سیئاتهم حسنات» (97)
  • Repentance is a cause for the sending down of rain. «توبوا ... یرسل السماء» (98)
  • Repentance is a cause for good provision. «توبوا الیه یمتّعكم متاعاً حسنا» (99)
  • Repentance is not accepted when one sees the signs of death and punishment. «حتّى اذا حضر احدهم الموت قال انّى تبت الان» (100)
  • In addition to accepting repentance, Allah also grants special favor. «هو التّواب الرحیم» (101) «ثمّ تاب و اصلح فانّه غفور رحیم» (102), «ثمّ تاب علیهم انّه بهم رؤف رحیم» (103) «الاّ مَن تاب و آمن و عمل صالحا فاولئك یدخلون الجنّة» (104) «ثمّ توبوا الیه ان ربّى رحیم ودود» (105) In these verses, alongside repentance, the issue of Divine mercy, compassion, and love is mentioned.
  • The Quran considers abandoning repentance to be oppression (ẓulm) and destruction. «و من لم یتب فاولئك هم الظالمون» (106) «ثم لم یتوبوا فلهم عذاب جهنّم» (107)
