Messages

  1. Sometimes future generations and offspring ruin the efforts of their fathers. (Sometimes unrighteous offspring emerge from righteous people.) «خَلْف اضاعوا الصلوة»
  2. Prayer is the axis of religion. (To show the character of one generation to another, the wasting of their prayer is highlighted.) «فخلف خَلْف اضاعوا الصلوة»
  3. Prayer is a barrier between man and his desires; if this barrier is broken, following desires becomes easy for man. «اضاعوا الصلوة واتّبعوا الشهوات»
  4. Whoever separates himself from the connection with Allah (prayer) becomes attached to desires. «اضاعوا الصلوة واتّبعوا الشهوات»
  5. The flourishing of desires is ghayy (error) and misguidance. «واتّبعوا الشهوات... یلقون غیّا»
  6. The punishment for wasting the prayer is confusion/bewilderment. «یلقون غیّا» (True guidance (rushd) is found in spirituality.)
  7. The destiny of nations is in their own hands. «اضاعوا... یلقون... الاّ من تاب...»
  8. Repentance is a transformation, not a mere pretense. «تاب و امن و عمل صالحاً»
  9. The condition for salvation and entry into Paradise is repentance, faith, and righteous deeds. «تاب و آمن و عمل صالحا... یدخلون الجنّة»

Ayahs

جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ٱلَّتِى وَعَدَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ عِبَادَهُۥ بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ وَعْدُهُۥ مَأْتِيًّا (٦۱)
Gardens of Eden promised by the All-beneficent to His servants, [while they were still] unseen. Indeed His promise is bound to come to pass. (61)
لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا إِلَّا سَلَـٰمًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ رِزْقُهُمْ فِيهَا بُكْرَةً وَعَشِيًّا (٦۲)
Therein they will not hear vain talk, but only ‘Peace!’ Therein they will have their provision morning and evening. (62)
تِلْكَ ٱلْجَنَّةُ ٱلَّتِى نُورِثُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا مَن كَانَ تَقِيًّا (٦۳)
This is the paradise We will give as inheritance to those of Our servants who are Godwary. (63)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the word jannah (جَنَّة) was used in the singular form, "یدخلون الجنّة" (they enter the Garden), but in this Ayah, the word jannāt (جَنَّات) is presented in the plural form. This indicates that the gardens and orchards of the people of Paradise are a collection of multiple orchards. This expression might be due to the fact that each person in Paradise possesses four gardens, as alluded to in Surah Ar-Rahman: "و لمَن خاف مقام ربّه جنّتان... و من دونهما جنّتان" (108) meaning, for everyone who is pious, there are two gardens, and after describing those two gardens, He says: besides these two gardens, there are two other gardens, totaling four gardens. These two (latter) gardens are either closer, or they are of a lower level, or the type of gardens differs due to the varying ranks of the people of Paradise. (109)

The Holy Quran has repeatedly spoken about the fulfillment of divine promises, including His statement: "و مَن اوفى بعهده من اللّه" (110) Who is more faithful to His covenant than Allah?

The praise of Paradise—that they hear no vain talk therein—is both a recommendation for us today to avoid vanity (laghw), and an indication that the idle speech of the disbelievers and the things heard that distressed the believers in this world will not be present there.

In the phrase "نورث مَن كان تقیّا" (We shall make those who are pious inherit it), taqwa (piety) is mentioned, which is the key to Paradise, and the term inheritance (irth) is used, which means attaining wealth and blessings without hardship or effort. Although piety is the key to Paradise, all those rewards are an inheritance from Allah to us, and our deeds are not sufficient for such a reward. In reality, our rewards are an inheritance that reaches us from Allah without hardship.

We read in a hadith: Every human being has a place in Paradise and a place in Hellfire. If he becomes one of the people of Paradise, his place in Hellfire is inherited by the people of Hellfire; and if he becomes one of the people of Hellfire, his place in Paradise is inherited by the people of Paradise. (111)

A person complained to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام about his stomach pain. The Imam told him: Eat two meals, morning and evening, and do not eat between them, as that causes corruption in the body. He then recited this Ayah: "رزقهم فیها بكرة وعشیّا" (112)

108) Ar-Rahman, 46 - 62. 109) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar. 110) At-Tawbah, 111. 111) Nur ath-Thaqalayn, Vol. 2, p. 31. 112) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn, Mahasin Barqi.

Messages

1- Residence in the gardens of Paradise is eternal. "جنّات عدن" (113)

2- The source of all those promised favors is the grace of Allah, the Most Compassionate (Ar-Rahman). "وعد الرحمن"

3- Today, we do not comprehend the reality of Allah’s promises. "بالغیب"

4- The common speech of the people of Paradise is salām (peace). "لایسمعون... الا سلاما" The environment of Paradise is safe and sound, and the people of Paradise hear salām both from the angels, "سلام علیكم طبتم" (114), and from their friends. "فسلام لك من اصحاب الیمین" (115)

5- In Paradise, there is temporal variation (morning and evening). "بكرة و عشیّا"

6- In Paradise, there is both spiritual security, "الاّ سلاما" (except peace), and the provision of physical needs. "رزقهم بكرة و عشیّا"

7- In the granting of Paradise, there is no reversal or revocation; this is the strongest type of ownership. (116) "نورث"

8- Paradise is the inheritance of the pious. "نورث من عبادنا مَن كان تقیّا"

113) The word ‘adn (عدن) means residence/abode.

114) Az-Zumar, 73.

115) Al-Waqi'ah, 91.

116) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar.

Ayah

وَمَا نَتَنَزَّلُ إِلَّا بِأَمْرِ رَبِّكَ ۖ لَهُۥ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِينَا وَمَا خَلْفَنَا وَمَا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ نَسِيًّا (٦٤)
[O Gabriel, tell the Prophet,] ‘We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us and whatever is behind us and whatever is in between that, and your Lord does not forget (64)

Key Points

In Tafsir al-Mizan, we read: This Ayah is independent, although some have tried to connect it with the preceding Ayahs. In the Tafsirs of Fakhr al-Razi, Tabari, and Tibyan, we read: Revelation was cut off from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله for a period, and this caused distress to the Prophet and mockery from the opponents. The Ayah was revealed: Your Lord is not forgetful. «ماكان ربّك نسیّا» Similar to the Ayah: «ما وَدّعك ربّك و ما قَلى»(117)

117) Duha, 3.

Messages

1- The revelation of the Quran is gradual and based on Allah's command. «و ما نتنزّل الاّ بامر ربّك»

2- The angels are submissive to the command of Allah. «و ما نتنزّل الاّ بامر ربّك»

3- He is the true Owner of everything. «له»

4- The delay in revelation was by the command of Allah, not because He had forgotten. (118) «ما كان ربّك نسیّا»

118) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran; Tibyan.

Ayah

رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فَٱعْبُدْهُ وَٱصْطَبِرْ لِعِبَـٰدَتِهِۦ ۚ هَلْ تَعْلَمُ لَهُۥ سَمِيًّا (٦٥)
—the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them. So worship Him and be steadfast in His worship. Do you know anyone who could be His namesake?’ (65)

Key Points

Several meanings have been mentioned for the word samiyy (سمىّ): partner, likeness, namesake, and son. The word Rabbuka (ربّك) in the previous Ayah and the word Rabbus-samāwāti wal-arḍ (ربّ السموات و الارض) in this Ayah indicate the unity of management in administering human life alongside the entire universe. Worship (‘ibādah) is not merely the remembrance of Allah and prayer (ṣalāh), because these types of worship do not pose a difficulty that would necessitate patience and resistance. Therefore, the intent of fa‘budhu waṣṭabir (فاعبده و اصطبر) is the worship that subjects man to conflicts, tyrants (ṭāghūts), and numerous difficulties, and for this very reason, requires patience (ṣabr) and steadfastness. In a narration from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, we read that regarding the meaning of Hal ta‘lamu lahu samiyyā (هل تعلم له سمیّا), He said: No one has ever been named by the name of Allah. (119)

119) Tawhid Saduq, p. 263.

Messages

1- The philosophy behind our worship of Allah is His exclusive Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) over the entire universe. Rabbus-samāwāti... fa‘bud (ربّ السّموات ... فاعبد)

2- Worship requires patience. Fa‘budhu waṣṭabir (فاعبده واصطبر). (Worship is a type of struggle against desires and tyrants (ṭāghūts), which requires patience.)

3- Allah has not forgotten you, so you must not forget Him either. Wa mā kāna Rabbuka nasīyā... fa‘budhu (وما كان ربّك نسیّاً... فاعبده)

4- Allah has no son, and no being is like Him. Hal ta‘lamu lahu samiyyā (هل تعلم له سمیّاً)

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ أَءِذَا مَا مِتُّ لَسَوْفَ أُخْرَجُ حَيًّا (٦٦)
Man says, ‘What? Shall I be brought forth alive [from the grave], when I have been dead?’ (66)
أَوَلَا يَذْكُرُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ أَنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰهُ مِن قَبْلُ وَلَمْ يَكُ شَيْـًٔا (٦۷)
Does not man remember that We created him before when he was nothing? (67)

Key Points

This Ayah is similar to Ayahs 78 and 79 of Surah Ya Sin, where a person brought a decayed bone, crushed it before the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, and engaged in discussion with the Prophet, saying: Who can revive decayed bones? Allah replied, (O Prophet!) Say: The same One who created it the first time will revive it after death, and He knows all things. «وضرب لنا مثلاً و نسى خلقه قال مَن یُحیى العِظام و هى رَمیم . قل یُحیها الّذى اَنشأها اوّل مَرّة و هو بكلّ خلق علیم»

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about «خلقناه من قبل و لم یك شیئاً» (We created him before, when he was nothing), and the Imam replied: It means that there was nothing, neither at the stage of predestination (taqdir) nor at the stage of creation (takwin). The narrator of the hadith asked about «لم یكن شیئاً مذكورا» (120) (He was a thing unremembered), and the Imam replied: It means it was predestined, but there was no mention of it. (121) Therefore, these two Ayahs speak of two stages preceding the creation of man. In another hadith, Imam Baqir عليه السلام also said regarding «من قبل» (from before): There was no mention of him, neither in the Book nor in knowledge. (122)

120) Al-Insan, 1. 121) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 147. 122) Bihar, Vol. 5, p. 84.

Messages

1- Lack of belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a way to justify sin and escape worship. «فاعبده و اصطبر لعبادته... یقول الانسان...»

2- Astonishment regarding the Resurrection and being revived again is not limited to a specific time or a particular group of people. «یقول الانسان»

3- The Resurrection is physical (corporeal). «اُخرج» means the body exiting the earth.

4- The Resurrection is compulsory and inevitable. «اُخرج» means an inevitable and involuntary exit.

5- Doubt in the Resurrection, considering one's initial creation, is astonishing. «أولایذكر»

6- Man's attention to the fact that he did not exist initially and was created later is sufficient for accepting the Resurrection. «أولا یذكر الانسان...»

7- Deniers of the Resurrection have no proof for their belief and merely consider the Day of Judgment improbable, but the Quran speaks with evidence. «خلقناه من قبل»

8- The creation of man from nothingness is far more complex and seemingly difficult than the renewed creation of man after death. «خلقناه... و لم یك شیئا»

Ayah

فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ وَٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ ثُمَّ لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيًّا (٦۸)
By your Lord, We will surely gather them with the devils; then We will surely bring them up around hell [scrambling] on their knees. (68)

Key Points

The word jithiyyā (جِثیّا), which is the plural of jāthin (جاثى), refers to someone who is kneeling for judgment, or it is derived from jathwah (جَثوة), meaning accumulated stones and earth. This signifies the worst type of sitting. In most instances in the Qur'an, Allah swears by His creation, but concerning the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), He swears by His own Holy Essence (123). "فَوَرَبِّكَ"

123) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- We must take the warnings of Qiyamah seriously. The gathering (Hashr) on the Day of Judgment is certain. "فَوَرَبِّكَ"

2- Allah consoles His Prophet against the disbelievers who do not accept the Resurrection. "فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ"

3- Satan is shoulder-to-shoulder with man in his disbelief. "لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ وَ الشَّيَاطِينَ" (The gathering of humans and devils together on the Day of Judgment is based on their shared ideology, affinity, and cooperation.)

4- Presence near Hell is a form of fear and torment, and falling into it is another punishment. "لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ"

5- On the Day of Judgment, the deviated will be in the worst condition: beside the devils, surrounding Hell, in a kneeling and humiliated state. "لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيًّا"

Ayahs

ثُمَّ لَنَنزِعَنَّ مِن كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ أَيُّهُمْ أَشَدُّ عَلَى ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ عِتِيًّا (٦۹)
Then from every group We shall draw whichever of them was more defiant towards the All-beneficent. (69)
ثُمَّ لَنَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ أَوْلَىٰ بِهَا صِلِيًّا (۷٠)
Then surely We will know best those who deserve most to enter it. (70)

Key Points

The word "شیعة" (Shī‘ah) means both the followers of a person and a group that agrees upon a certain path or school of thought. "عِتیّا" (‘Itiyyā) means rebellion and defiance. "صِلیّا" (Ṣiliyyā) means lighting a fire, burning with fire, and entering the fire.

Messages

1- Sinners will be separated and categorized on the Day of Judgment. «لننزعنّ من كلّ شیعة...» (Those who rebel more against the Most Compassionate God will have a specific and more severe reckoning and punishment.)

2- Transgressors are three groups: a group that commits the transgression personally, a group that becomes the means and intermediary for others to commit transgression, and individuals who are pleased with the transgression of others. It is obvious that the sin and punishment of each of these three groups differ. «أیّهم أشدّ ...»

3- Although Allah is the source of all mercies, man is defiant towards Him. «على الرّحمن عتیّا»

4- The punishment of Hell has a calculated and precise system, and Allah's knowledge guarantees that precision and differentiation. «ثمّ لننزعنّ... أیّهم أشدّ... لنحن أعلم...»

5- Hell has different levels and depths, and it will vary according to the severity and weakness of the sin, and the degrees of deservingness of each person are not hidden from God. «لنحن أعلم»

Ayah

وَإِن مِّنكُمْ إِلَّا وَارِدُهَا ۚ كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ حَتْمًا مَّقْضِيًّا (۷۱)
There is none of you but will come to it: a [matter that is a] decided certainty with your Lord. (71)

Key Points

It is narrated in Tafsir Tabari: One day, Abdullah ibn Rawahah was weeping while ill. Seeing him, his wife also began to weep. Abdullah asked: Why are you shedding tears? She replied: Because of your weeping. Abdullah said: But I weep because of this saying of Allah, the Exalted, Who said: «اِن منكم الاّ واردها» (There is none of you but shall reach it). Our entry into Hell is certain, but I do not know whether I will be saved from it or not.

This Ayah must be interpreted alongside the verses and narrations related to the Sirat (the Path), because the Sirat is a bridge stretched over Hell, and all people will cross over it—some quickly, some slowly, and some will fall into Hell.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: On the Day of Resurrection, all human beings (both good and bad) will enter Hell, but the fire will not harm the believers, just as the fire became cool for Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham).

Therefore, the Ayah under discussion, which deems the entry of everyone into Hell certain, does not contradict the verses that state believers are kept far away from Hell (124). This is because, on the one hand, they enter Hell (by crossing the bridge), and on the other hand, by the grace of Allah, they suffer no harm.

Of course, it is possible to say that wurud (entry/reaching) means crossing, not entering inside. Just as it is mentioned regarding Prophet Musa عليه السلام: «ورد ماء مَدین» (He came to the water of Madyan), meaning He reached the edge of the water of the city of Madyan, not that He entered the water (125).

Or we might say: The intent of the word «منكم» (of you) in the Ayah under discussion refers to the groups mentioned in the preceding verses, meaning the followers of Satan. And the intent of saving the righteous (the Muttaqin) is saving those who have been saved from disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk). That is, all followers of Satan will enter Hell, but the disbelievers and polytheists will remain there, while sinners who were not tainted by polytheism and disbelief, but were wicked (fasiq), will eventually be freed.

124) «اولئك عنها مبعدون» (Those will be kept far away from it [the Fire]). The believers are kept far away from the fire of Hell. Al-Anbiya, 101. «و هم من فزع یومئذ آمنون» (And they will be safe from the terror of that Day). The righteous are safe from the terror of the Day of Resurrection. An-Naml, 89. «لایسمعون حسیسها» (They will not hear its sound). The righteous believers will not hear the sound of the fire of Hell. Al-Anbiya, 102. 125) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Have you not heard a person who says: "I entered the water of such-and-such tribe," meaning he reached the water, not that he entered into the water. Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Resurrection and its stages are calculated and precise. «لنحشرنّهم... لننزعنّ... اِن منكم الاّ واردها»

2- Allah possesses definite and certain laws and programs. «كان على ربّك حتماًمقضیّا»

Ayah

ثُمَّ نُنَجِّى ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوا۟ وَّنَذَرُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ فِيهَا جِثِيًّا (۷۲)
Then We will deliver those who are Godwary, and leave the wrongdoers in it, fallen on their knees. (72)

Key Points

| It is narrated from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that he said: All people will enter the Fire, then they will exit it according to the measure of their deeds; a group will exit the Fire like lightning, a group like the wind, a group like a horse, a group like a man running, and a group like ordinary walking. (126)

126) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Hell is the passage for the pious and the dwelling place for the oppressors. «اِن منكم الاّ واردها... ثمّ ننجّى الّذین اتّقوا و نذر الظالمین فیها»

2- Piety (Taqwa) is the sole path to salvation from Hell. «ثم ننجّى الّذین اتّقوا»

3- The impious person is an oppressor (Zalim). «و نذر الظالمین»

4- Oppressors deserve humiliation. «نذر الظالمین» (This phrase is used when something is abandoned due to its worthlessness and lowliness.)

Ayah

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَىُّ ٱلْفَرِيقَيْنِ خَيْرٌ مَّقَامًا وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا (۷۳)
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, the faithless say to the faithful, ‘Which of the two groups is superior in station and better with respect to company?’ (73)

Key Points

Nadā (نَدىّ) refers to any gathering or a consultative assembly. Dār al-Nadwah (دار النَدوة) means the consultative council. «و تأتون فى نادیكم المنكر»(127) means you commit sin in your assemblies. «فلیدع نادیه»(128) means let him call his counselors. The disbelievers, either mockingly or in the form of a question, would say: Do wealthy individuals like Nadhr ibn Harith and Walid ibn Mughirah have value and status, or do the poor ones like Bilal and Ammar? And with this arrogant view, they separated themselves from the Muslims and refused to believe. Indeed, some individuals try to compensate for their spiritual deficiencies with material displays. As mentioned in some narrations: The disbelieving nobles would wear beautiful and luxurious clothes and walk in front of the Prophet and his companions, looking at them with contempt.(129)

Do not look upon the humble ones of the world with contempt; If you see a handle-less jug, lift it with both hands.

127) Al-Ankabut, 29. 128) Al-Alaq, 17. 129) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi.

Messages

1- Believers must be prepared to respond to the propaganda pressure and doubts raised by the enemies. «قال الّذین كفروا للّذین آمنوا»

2- The matter of correct belief and logic is separate from worldly prosperity. «اىّ الفریقَین خیرٌ» The disbelievers would say: If our path is false, why is our worldly situation better than yours?

3- Money and wealth are not signs of righteousness or character. «اىّ الفریقین خیرٌ مقاما»

4- Boasting and pride pave the way for disbelief. «قال الّذین كفروا... اىّ الفریقین خیرٌ»

Ayah

وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَحْسَنُ أَثَـٰثًا وَرِءْيًا (۷٤)
How many a generation We have destroyed before them, who were superior in furnishings and appearance! (74)

Key Points

The word athāth (أَثَاث) means the means of living and welfare. Ri'yā (رِءیا) refers to visible scenes or sights. Qarn (قَرْن) is said of people who live together at the same time (a generation/era).

Messages

1- The history of the downfall of tyrants and disbelievers is a source of human knowledge and a lesson for reflection. «و كم اهلكنا قبلهم» (History is an interconnected collection from whose past one can take lessons for the future.)

2- The number of disbelievers and tyrants who have been destroyed is not small. «و كم اهلكنا»

3- Historical transformations follow the traditions (Sunan) that Allah has decreed. «كم اهلكنا»

4- Welfare facilities and material possessions are neither a sign of felicity nor do they prevent the wrath of Allah. «اهلكنا... هم احسن اثاثاً»

5- Welfare and facilities are factors leading to arrogance, heedlessness, transgression, and ultimately, destruction. «كم اهلكنا... احسن اثاثاً و رءیا»

Ayah

قُلْ مَن كَانَ فِى ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ مَدًّا ۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَأَوْا۟ مَا يُوعَدُونَ إِمَّا ٱلْعَذَابَ وَإِمَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ هُوَ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا وَأَضْعَفُ جُندًا (۷٥)
Say, ‘Whoever abides in error, the All-beneficent shall prolong his respite until they sight what they have been promised: either punishment, or the Hour.’ Then they will know whose position is worse, and whose host is weaker. (75)

Key Points

Mad (مَدّ) and Imdad (امداد) share the same meaning, but according to Raghib, Imdad is usually used for desirable and beloved actions, while Mad is used for undesirable and reprehensible actions. Allah the Almighty grants respite to all those who choose the path of evil. This respite is granted so that perhaps they may repent, or righteous children may emerge from their lineage. Although some misguided individuals misuse the divine respite, committing more sins and thereby increasing their punishment.(130)

The grace of the Truth (Allah) deals gently with you, But when it exceeds the limit, He exposes (you).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "اذا رأوا ما یوعدون" (when they see what they are promised): That promise is the advent (Zuhur) of Hazrat Qa'im عليه السلام, and "شرّ مكاناً" (worst in position) means that on the day of the Qa'im's uprising, it will become clear who is in an undesirable and weak position.(131)

130) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan. 131) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 431.

Messages

1- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to provide the opportunity for movement and growth for all human beings. If a person chooses misguidance, Allah grants him respite for a period, "مَن كان فى الضلالة فلیَمدد" (Whoever is in error, let the Most Gracious extend [it] to him), and if he takes the path of guidance, Allah increases his guidance. "و یزیداللّه الّذین اهتدوا هدىً" (And Allah increases those who are guided, in guidance).

2- Respite and aid stem from Divine Mercy. "فلیَمدُد له الرّحمن" (Let the Most Gracious extend [it] to him).

3- The disbelievers were repeatedly threatened with danger and punishment, but they did not take heed. "مایوعدون" (what they are promised).

4- Allah punishes some misguided individuals in this world while their reckoning for the Day of Judgment remains, but He punishes others only on the Day of Judgment. "اِمّا العذاب واِمّا السّاعة" (either the punishment or the Hour).

5- At the moment of death, status, position, relatives, and followers are of no use. "شرٌّ مكاناً و اضعف جنداً" (worst in position and weakest in forces).

6- The Day of Judgment is the day when realities emerge and truths are revealed and witnessed. " حتّى اذا رأوا... فسیعلمون..." (until when they see... then they will know...).

Ayah

وَيَزِيدُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱهْتَدَوْا۟ هُدًى ۗ وَٱلْبَـٰقِيَـٰتُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتُ خَيْرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ مَّرَدًّا (۷٦)
Allah enhances in guidance those who are [rightly] guided, and lasting righteous deeds are better with your Lord in reward, and better at the return [to Allah]. (76)

Key Points

The word "مَرَدّ" (maradd) means outcome (consequence) and benefit. The word "ثواب" (thawāb) means the recompense for an action that returns to a person, whether it is good or evil; however, it is mostly used for good (deeds). (132) In the previous Ayah, we read that the misguided fall into deeper misguidance. This Ayah states that those who are guided also attain greater guidance and illumination. In some narrations, we read that the meaning of "الباقیات الصّالحات" (the enduring good deeds) is the remembrance (dhikr) of "سبحان اللّه و الحمدللّه و لا اله الاّاللّه واللّه اكبر." (133)

132) Tafsir al-Mizan. 133) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Guidance has stages and degrees. "و یزیداللّه ..." (And Allah increases...)

2- Righteous deeds are preserved with Allah. "والباقیات الصّالحات خیر عند ربّك" (And the enduring good deeds are better with your Lord)

3- The success (Tawfīq) in performing worthy deeds is due to Divine guidance. "اهتدوا هُدى و الباقیات الصّالحات" (those who are guided, guidance, and the enduring good deeds)

4- Allah has special favor toward His Prophet. "عند ربّك" (with your Lord)

5- Punishment and reward are among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). "عند ربّك ثواباً" (with your Lord as a reward)

6- Good deeds are the key to a favorable outcome. "الباقیات الصّالحات... خیر مردّا" (the enduring good deeds... better in outcome)

Ayahs

أَفَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى كَفَرَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَقَالَ لَأُوتَيَنَّ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا (۷۷)
Have you not regarded him who defies Our signs, and says, ‘I will surely be given wealth and children’? (77)
أَطَّلَعَ ٱلْغَيْبَ أَمِ ٱتَّخَذَ عِندَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ عَهْدًا (۷۸)
Has he come to know the Unseen, or taken a promise from the All-beneficent? (78)
كَلَّا ۚ سَنَكْتُبُ مَا يَقُولُ وَنَمُدُّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ مَدًّا (۷۹)
No indeed! We will write down what he says, and We will prolong his punishment endlessly. (79)
وَنَرِثُهُۥ مَا يَقُولُ وَيَأْتِينَا فَرْدًا (۸٠)
We shall take over from him what he talks about, and he will come to Us alone. (80)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of "أفرأیت الّذى كفر" (Have you seen him who disbelieved): 'As ibn Wa'il owed Khabbab a debt. Khabbab came to him to collect his due, but 'As said: "Do you not believe that there is gold, silver, and silk in Paradise?" Khabbab said: "Yes." 'As said: "Then our appointment is in Paradise, and I swear that in Paradise you will be given better than what you gave me."(134)

134) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 2, p. 53.

Messages

1- Abandoning Allah and seeking refuge in wealth and children is astonishing. "أفرایت" (Have you seen).

2- The disbeliever considers religion an obstacle to progress and disbelief a factor for growth. "كفر بایاتنا وقال لاوتینّ" (He disbelieved in Our signs and said, "I will surely be given").

3- The criterion of value among the disbelievers is wealth and children. "لاوتینّ مالاً و ولداً" (I will surely be given wealth and children). (The disbeliever, when faced with logic and threat, is only content with what he possesses.)

4- Disdain the misguided affluent so that they do not become role models in society. "أفرأیت... اطّلع الغیب... نمدّ له من العذاب" (Have you seen... Has he looked into the unseen... We will extend for him the punishment).

5- Allah has not signed a contract with anyone. "ام اتّخذ عندالرّحمن عَهدا" (Or has he taken a covenant from the Most Merciful).

6- Although Allah's covenant is never broken, the disbelievers have not made a covenant with Allah. "ام اتّخذ عند الرّحمن عهدا" (Or has he taken a covenant from the Most Merciful).

7- Suppress false notions explicitly. "كلاّ" (No!).

8- The actions and words of man are recorded in the Divine Register. "سنكتب ما یقول" (We will record what he says).

9- There is a period of respite for every word and deed, perhaps the person will regret it and repent. But if that period ends and he does not regret it, it is recorded. The letter 'س' (Sīn) in "سنكتب" (We will record) is a sign of respite.(135)

10- Speech must rely on knowledge and evidence, because man is responsible for what he says. "سنكتب ما قالوا" (We will record what they said).

11- On the Day of Judgment, man abandons the wealth and children that caused his disbelief and arrogance, and he appears before Allah alone. "یأتینا فرداً" (He will come to Us alone).

135) We also read in narrations: As soon as a person commits a bad deed, the angels are not commanded to record it for a period, perhaps he will regret it, but after the period ends, it is recorded.

Ayahs

وَٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ءَالِهَةً لِّيَكُونُوا۟ لَهُمْ عِزًّا (۸۱)
They have taken gods besides Allah that they may be a [source of] might to them. (81)
كَلَّا ۚ سَيَكْفُرُونَ بِعِبَادَتِهِمْ وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضِدًّا (۸۲)
No Indeed! Soon they will disown their worship, and they will be their opponents. (82)

Key Points

According to Allamah Tabataba'i, the phrase "كلاّ سیكفرون" (No, they will deny) has two interpretations: A: The polytheists will deny their own worship and become opposed to the idols. B: The tyrants (Taghuts) and idols, instead of interceding, granting honor, or helping their worshippers, will rise up against them. Other verses in the Qur'an also point to this meaning. (136)

"عَزیز" (Aziz) means the Victorious and Impenetrable One Whom no harm or evil can reach.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of "آلهة" (gods/deities) are the leaders whom people obey in search of honor (Izzah). (137)

Of course, the opposition of deities like the tyrants (Taghuts) and devils to their followers is clear, but deities like stone, wood, and others may, by the will of Allah, speak on the Day of Judgment and disavow the polytheists and their worshippers. (138)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this verse: The meaning of worship ('Ibadah) in the verse is not prostration (Sujud) and bowing (Ruku'), but rather obedience and submission. He said: «مَن اطاع مخلوقاً فى معصیة الخالق فقد عَبَده» (Whoever obeys a creature in disobedience to the Creator has worshipped him). Whoever commits a sin by obeying a creature, that obedience is considered worship. (139)

(136) «یكفرون بشرككم» (The deities will deny your polytheism). Fatir, 14. «و اذا حشر الناس كانوا لهم اعداء و كانوا بعبادتهم كافرین» (And when mankind are gathered, they will be enemies to them and will deny their worship). Al-Ahqaf, 6. (137) Tafsir Safi. (138) Tafsir Namuneh. (139) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The desire for honor (Izzah) is inherent in human nature, but man errs in its application and does not seek the true source of honor. «اتّخذوا من دون اللّه ...»

2- The Day of Judgment is near; let us believe in it. «سیكفرون»

3- Those very beings upon whom man relied instead of Allah will rise up against him. «ویكونون علیهم ضدّا» (Reliance on other than Allah leads to their enmity on the Day of Judgment.)

Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّا (۸۳)
Have you not regarded that We unleash the devils upon the faithless to urge them vigorously? (83)
فَلَا تَعْجَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ إِنَّمَا نَعُدُّ لَهُمْ عَدًّا (۸٤)
So do not make haste against them; indeed We are counting for them, a counting [down]. (84)

Key Points

The word azz (أَزّ) means intense incitement and uprooting. Azz al-Qidr (أَزّ القِدر) means the pot boiled so intensely that everything inside it was turned upside down. The relationship of Satan with the people of faith is simple, followed by vigilance and repentance. (Waswasa ilayh) (140) But the relationship of Satan with the disbelievers is a relationship of dominance. (Arsalnā ash-Shayāṭīna ‘alā al-Kāfirīn) (We sent the devils upon the disbelievers), not (ilā al-Kāfirīn) (to the disbelievers). Of course, this is a dominance for which the disbeliever himself has prepared the ground. (Innamā sulṭānuhu ‘alā alladhīna yatawallawnahu) (141) It is possible that the meaning of Allah sending the devils is actually His letting them loose; for example, if someone does not tie up his dog or prevent it, they say he "sent" his dog. It is stated in Tafsir Qummi: The Ayah (Wa arsalnā ash-Shayāṭīn) was revealed concerning those who do not pay their Khums (one-fifth) and Zakat (alms). Allah sends a sulṭān (authority) or a devil upon them so that they spend the amount of wealth they failed to pay for Khums and Zakat in ways other than the way of Allah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Even a person's parents can count the days of his life; therefore, the meaning of (na‘uddu lahum ‘addā) is that We know the number of their breaths as well. (142)

140) Taha, 120. 141) An-Nahl, 100. 142) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

  1. Those who chose disbelief and accepted the guardianship (wilāyah) of Satan, Allah sends the devils to them to encourage and incite them to corruption. (One of the punishments for disbelievers is the dominance of Satan over them.) (Innā arsalnā ash-Shayāṭīna ‘alā al-Kāfirīn)
  2. Satan incites man, but he does not compel him. (Ta’uzzuhum azzā)
  3. Satan's incitement is very strong. (Ta’uzzuhum azzā)
  4. Nothing, not even the whisperings of Satan, is outside the Will of Allah. (Innā arsalnā... ta’uzzuhum azzā)
  5. Disbelief is the cause of susceptibility to Satan's incitement. (‘Alā al-Kāfirīn ta’uzzuhum)
  6. The knowledge of the prophets regarding interests (masālih) and events is limited. (Falā ta‘jal ‘alayhim)
  7. Persistence in disbelief and enmity compelled the Prophet, who possessed great character (khuluq ‘aẓīm) and forbearance, to request punishment for them. (Falā ta‘jal ‘alayhim)
  8. Allah's tradition (Sunnah) is to grant respite to people. (Na‘uddu lahum ‘addā) Divine plans are organized and timed, so do not hasten the death and destruction of the disbelievers. (Falā ta‘jal ‘alayhim)
  9. State your commands, prohibitions, and instructions with justification. (Falā ta‘jal... innamā na‘uddu lahum)

Ayahs

يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ وَفْدًا (۸٥)
The day We shall gather the Godwary toward the All-beneficent, on mounts, (85)
وَنَسُوقُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ وِرْدًا (۸٦)
and drive the guilty as a thirsty herd towards hell, (86)

Key Points

The term «وَفد» refers to a group of newcomers who arrive mounted (on animals/vehicles) for pilgrimage (Ziyarah) or to seek a need. The word «وِرد» means thirst.

Messages

1- The scenes of the Day of Resurrection are worthy of remembrance. The Day of Resurrection is the day when the difference between the righteous (Muttaqin) and the criminals (Mujrimin) becomes manifest. «و یوم...»

2- The manner of the presence of the people of Paradise and the entry of the people of Hell is very important. «وَفداً... وِرداً»

3- The pious are the guests of Allah, «وَفداً», but the criminals are driven forth in humiliation. «نَسوق». The righteous (Muttaqin) enter Paradise with special honors and the welcome of the angels.(143)

4- More important than Paradise is Divine Mercy, of which Paradise is but one manifestation. «الى الرّحمن» is used instead of «الى الجنّة».

5- The key to Paradise is Divine piety (Taqwa). «نحشر المتّقین الى الرّحمن»

Ayah

لَّا يَمْلِكُونَ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةَ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ عِندَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ عَهْدًا (۸۷)
no one will have the power to intercede [with Allah], except for him who has taken a covenant with the All-beneficent. (87)

Key Points

The Quran stipulates conditions for intercession (Shafa'ah); it is not the case that anyone can choose whatever they wish as their intercessor. On the Day of Resurrection, the disbelievers will knock on every door and seek refuge from everyone, but they will receive a negative answer. It is possible that the intent of the "covenant" ('ahd) is the covenant of servitude to Allah and distance from Satan, concerning which the Quran states: «ألم اَعهَد الیكم یا بنى آدم أن لا تعبدوا الشیطان اِنّه لكم عدوّ مبین و أنِ اعبدونى هذا صراط مستقیم»(144) O humans! Did I not take a covenant from you that you should not obey Satan, for he is a manifest enemy to you, and that you should worship Me alone, for this is the Straight Path? And in another place, we read: «لا تَنفع الشفاعة الاّ مَن أذن له الرّحمن و رَضِى له قولا»(145) On that Day, the intercession of no one will benefit except for him whom the All-Merciful has permitted and whose word He is pleased with. In narrations, the "covenant" ('ahd) has been interpreted as several things, including: A: Commitment to the Wilayah (guardianship) of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام and the Imams after him.(146) B: The will made at the time of death, where a person gathers people around him and says: I am committed to "Lā ilāha illā Allāh, Muḥammad Rasūl Allāh," and the truthfulness of Paradise and Hell.(147) C: Commitment to and observance of the daily prayers.(148)

143) A detailed explanation of this ceremony has been stated in a narration from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 95. 144) Ya Sin, 60 - 61. 145) Ta Ha, 109. 146) Tafsir al-Mizan. 147) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 148) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Intercession (Shafa'ah) possesses a lawful system and is under the supervision of Allah. «لایملكون الشفاعة الاّ مَن...»

2- No one possesses the power or right of intercession arbitrarily or on their own accord. «لا یملكون»

3- Intercession is a manifestation of the expansive Divine Mercy. «عند الرّحمن عهدا»

4- Specific and committed individuals have the right of intercession from Allah. «الاّ مَن اتّخذ عند الرّحمن عهدا»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ وَلَدًا (۸۸)
They say, ‘The All-beneficent has taken a son!’ (88)
لَّقَدْ جِئْتُمْ شَيْـًٔا إِدًّا (۸۹)
You have certainly advanced something hideous! (89)

Key Points

The word "idd" (اِدّ) means a great and extremely repulsive denial (or monstrosity). This Ayah includes many groups, such as: A: The polytheists (Mushrikīn), who considered the angels to be the daughters of Allah. «واتّخذ من الملائكة اناثاً» (149) B: The Jews, who believed Ezra (Uzayr) was the son of Allah. «و قالتِ الیهود عُزیر ابن اللّه» (150) C: The Christians (Naṣārā), who counted the Messiah (Christ) as the son of Allah. «قالتِ النّصارى المسیح ابن اللّه» (151)

149) Isra, 40. 150) Tawbah, 30. 151) Tawbah, 30.

Messages

1- The belief that Allah has a son is a baseless and extremely repulsive statement. «ولداً...شیئاًاِدّا»

2- Allah having a son is not a sign of perfection, but rather a source of deficiency. «شیئاًاِدّا»

Ayahs

تَكَادُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ وَتَنشَقُّ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَتَخِرُّ ٱلْجِبَالُ هَدًّا (۹٠)
The heavens are about to be rent apart at it, the earth to split open, and the mountains to collapse into bits, (90)
أَن دَعَوْا۟ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ وَلَدًا (۹۱)
that they should ascribe a son to the All-beneficent! (91)
وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا (۹۲)
It does not behoove the All-beneficent to take a son. (92)
إِن كُلُّ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّآ ءَاتِى ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ عَبْدًا (۹۳)
There is none in the heavens and the earth but he comes to the All-beneficent as a servant. (93)

Key Points

The word "هَدّ" means collapse or fall. The issue of nature's reaction to truths has been raised repeatedly in the Quran, including: In one place, He says: some stones fall from the mountain out of the fear of Allah. «و اِنّ منها لما یهبط من خشیة اللّه»(152) Or He says: If We had sent down this Quran upon a mountain, you would have certainly seen it humbled and split apart from the fear of Allah. «لو انزلنا هذا القرآن على جبلٍ لرأیته خاشعاً متصدّعاً من خَشیة اللّه»(153)

(152) Baqarah, 74. (153) Hashr, 21.

Messages

1- Sin can cause the disruption of the cosmic order. «تكاد السموات یتفطّرن منه»

2- Allah created existence for humanity, and if humanity deviates, the collapse of existence is not unwarranted. «تكاد السموات یتفطّرن منه»

3- The greatest corruption in the universe is corrupt belief. «دعوا للرّحمن ولدا»

4- Attributing a son to Allah implies that He needs the continuation of His lineage, requires help and support, and suffers from loneliness, whereas He is far removed from all these things. «ما ینبغى للرّحمن أن یتّخذ ولداً»

5- All existence is under the sovereignty of Allah and returns to Him. «آتى الرّحمن عبدا»

6- Existence and all its creatures are entirely under the command of Allah and are His servants; why do you call His servant His son? «و ما ینبغى... اِن كلّ... عبداً»

Ayahs

لَّقَدْ أَحْصَىٰهُمْ وَعَدَّهُمْ عَدًّا (۹٤)
Certainly He has counted them [all] and numbered them precisely, (94)
وَكُلُّهُمْ ءَاتِيهِ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فَرْدًا (۹٥)
and each of them will come to Him alone on the Day of Resurrection. (95)

Messages

1- The precise statistics of all components of existence are in His hand. «لقد احصاهم و عَدّهم عَدّا» The knowledge of Allah does not merely encompass generalities; rather, He knows the details as well.

Beware, for every particle is accounted for here; This is the court of reckoning and the Book is here.(154)

2- The beings in existence are limited. «عَدّهم عَدّا»

3- The Resurrection is for everyone. «كلّهم»

4- Although the gathering (Hashr) is for everyone, each person is concerned with himself, and it is as if man is utterly alone. «فَرداً», «لقد جئتمونا فُرادى كما خلقناكم اوّل مَرّة»(155)

5- On the Day of Resurrection, all familial, economic, social, partisan, and other dependencies will be severed. «فَرداً»

6- On the Day of Resurrection, wealth, children, friends, and so forth, will be of no benefit. «فَرداً»

154) Fayd Kashani.

155) Al-An'am, 94.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ سَيَجْعَلُ لَهُمُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ وُدًّا (۹٦)
Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—the All-beneficent will endear them [to His creation]. (96)

Key Points

We have often seen that whenever righteous and faithful people pass away, even careless and wicked individuals fall into sorrow, unconsciously mourn, shed tears, honor their memory, visit their graves, or recount their memories to others. All of this is due to the pure nature (fitrah) of humanity and its love for perfection. Indeed, whoever attaches his heart solely to Allah, Allah turns the hearts of the people toward him. Whoever remembers Allah, Allah remembers him. "فاذكرونى اَذكُركم" (So remember Me; I will remember you.) (156) It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: The Wilayah (guardianship/authority) of Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام is a reward that Allah grants to believers who perform righteous deeds. (157) And a similar narration has been reported from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله concerning the affection and love of the people of faith for Ali عليه السلام. (158)

156) Baqarah, 152. 157) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 431. 158) Tafsir Furat, p. 247.

Messages

1- Faith without action and action without faith are ineffective. "آمنوا و عملوا..." (They have believed and done righteous deeds...)

2- Faith and righteous work are the key to popularity among people. "اِنّ الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات سیجعل... وُدّا" (Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds—the Most Merciful will appoint for them affection.) Whenever we notice our popularity diminishing, we should analyze our belief or performance, for Allah's promise is never broken.

3- Becoming beloved is in the hands of Allah. "سیجعل لهم الرّحمن" (The Most Merciful will appoint for them). Hearts are also in His hands.

4- Becoming beloved is a Divine mercy bestowed upon the believers and the righteous. "سیجعل لهم الرّحمن" (The Most Merciful will appoint for them).

5- It is possible that your righteous work is not valued today, but your continuous patience and effort will bear fruit in the future. "سیجعل ..." (He will appoint...).

6- Popularity is among the Divine favors and rewards in this world. "سیجعل لهم الرّحمن وُدّا" (The Most Merciful will appoint for them affection). (Believers and the righteous receive rewards in this world in addition to the reward of the Hereafter.)

Ayah

فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرْنَـٰهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لِتُبَشِّرَ بِهِ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ وَتُنذِرَ بِهِۦ قَوْمًا لُّدًّا (۹۷)
Indeed We have made it simple in your language so that you may give good news thereby to the Godwary and warn with it a disputatious lot. (97)

Key Points

The word ludd (لُدّ) means intense enmity.

Messages

1- Speaking easily (making things easy to understand) is a Quranic method for guidance. Yassarnā (یَسّرنا). Of course, speaking easily is different from speaking weakly; speech must be firm but fluent. Uḥkimat āyātuh (اُحكمت آیاته) (159)

2- Fluent expression is among the Divine favors. Yassarnā bi-lisānik (یَسّرنا بلسانك)

3- One must speak to every group in a specific manner. Li-tubashshir... wa tunthir (لتبشّر ... و تُنذر) (With the pious (muttaqīn) using the language of glad tidings, and with the enemies in the form of warning/admonition (inthār).)

159) Hud, 1.

Ayah

وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزًۢا (۹۸)
How many a generation We have destroyed before them! Can you descry any one of them, or hear from them so much as a murmur? (98)

Key Points

This noble Ayah is a source of comfort and solace for the Holy Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله). It implicitly gives Him the good news that His stubborn enemies will soon be destroyed, just as many opponents of the truth were destroyed in the past. The word 'rikz' (رِكز) means a soft and hidden sound. Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام), in response to Jabir concerning the Ayah, said: The destroyed people are Banu Umayyah. You will not see any of them in a state where he has hope or is free from fear and terror. Jabir says: I asked, "Is this possible?" He replied: It will happen very soon. (160)

160) بحار، ج 46، ص 30.

Messages

1- The destruction of stubborn nations is a sign of the seriousness of the Quran's warnings. «و تُنذر به قوماً لُدّاً و كم اهلكنا»

2- The destruction of the enemies of the truth is one of the Divine traditions. «و كم اهلكنا»

3- History is a mirror of lessons. «هل تُحِسّ... او تَسمَع»
